research.what_it_is: UI-TARS is a native, end-to-end vision-language GUI agent model built by ByteDance on top of Qwen-2-VL (7B and 72B variants). It takes raw screenshots as its sole input and outputs structured mouse/keyboard actions, unifying perception, grounding, reasoning, and memory in a single fine-tuned model rather than a prompt-orchestrated framework. The desktop app (UI-TARS-desktop) is a TypeScript/Electron monorepo that wires the model to local OS input via the nut.js native library, exposes an operator SDK, and also serves as an MCP server for external orchestrators.
research.architecture: 1. CAPTURE: operator.screenshot() returns {base64: string, scaleFactor: number}. Local desktop uses @nut-tree/nut-js screen.grab(); browser uses Puppeteer page.screenshot(); remote uses REST GET /screenshot. 2. ENCODE: base64 image packed into an OpenAI-compatible chat message alongside the user instruction and the last N (thought, action, screenshot) tuples as history within a 32k token budget. 3. REASON+GROUND: model outputs structured text: 'Thought: [deliberation]\nAction: action_name(params)'. Coordinates embedded in action params as 0-1000 normalized integers. 4. PARSE: @ui-tars/action-parser runs parse_action_to_structure_output(response, factor=1000, origin_resized_height, origin_resized_width, model_type='qwen25vl') to extract PredictionParsed[]. Smart-resize rescaling maps 0-1000 coords to actual pixels: X_px = round(image_width * X_model / 1000). 5. EXECUTE: operator.execute(parsed) calls nut.js for local mouse/keyboard or POSTs to /action for remote. 6. LOOP: repeat until Finished() or CallUser() or max_iterations=25. StatusEnum: INIT -> RUNNING -> END / MAX_LOOP.
research.grounding_approach: Pure coordinate regression from screenshot pixels - no a11y tree, no DOM, no set-of-marks overlay. The model (Qwen-2-VL fine-tuned on 25M+ element-coordinate pairs) takes the raw screenshot image and outputs normalized [x, y] coordinates in a 0-1000 integer space directly in the action token sequence. Format: click(start_box='(473,318)') where 473 means 47.3% of image width. The action_parser then applies: actual_px = round(model_coord / 1000 * original_dimension). For bounding-box-annotated training elements, center = (x1+x2)//2, (y1+y2)//2 before normalization. No intermediate OCR, no UIA tree lookup, no set-of-marks image overlay - the VLM directly maps visual appearance to coordinate tokens. The GROUNDING prompt template strips the thought chain for coordinate-only output in evaluation mode.
research.action_space: Unified cross-platform function-call syntax in model output text. SHARED: click(start_box), drag(start_box, end_box), scroll(start_box, direction, step_count), type(content), wait(), finished(), call_user(). DESKTOP-ONLY: left_double(start_box), right_single(start_box), hotkey(key), keydown(key), keyup(key). MOBILE-ONLY: long_press(start_box), press_back(), press_home(), press_enter(). All coordinates as start_box='(x,y)' with 0-1000 normalized integers. Three prompt templates in codes/ui_tars/prompt.py: COMPUTER_USE (full desktop), MOBILE_USE (touch), GROUNDING (coordinate-only, no thought).
speed_tactics: W4A8 quantization cuts per-round latency from 4.0s to 2.5s and raises throughput from 29.6 to 47 tok/s with negligible accuracy loss.
GROUNDING prompt template skips thought generation entirely, saving 100-300 tokens per step for simple sub-tasks.
Context window truncated to last N screenshot-thought-action triples to fit 32k token budget; older context dropped, not summarized.
vLLM and SGLang OpenAI-compatible server for continuous batching across concurrent sessions.
Community GGUF quantized variants enable CPU/low-VRAM fully offline local inference.
No a11y tree fetch per step - pure screenshot pipeline eliminates one system call round-trip per action.
Docker model run (hf.co/ByteDance-Seed/UI-TARS-7B-DPO) for prebuilt inference container deployment.
standout_features: End-to-end native model: perception, grounding, action, and System-2 reasoning are all weights, not prompt scaffolding around a generic VLM.
System-2 deliberation baked into weights via thought augmentation on 6M GUI tutorials plus DPO on reflective trace pairs - the model plans and self-corrects within a single autoregressive sequence.

Reflective online trace collection: 100s of virtual machines run the agent, failures are filtered by rule-based heuristics plus VLM scoring, then distilled back into training data for continuous self-improvement.
DPO-based reflection training: error-correction pairs (mistake trace vs corrected recovery trace) teach the model to prefer recovery paths over error continuation.
25M+ element-coordinate grounding pairs: proprietary grounding dataset across web, mobile, and desktop. ScreenSpot-V2: 94.2%, ScreenSpotPro 61.6% (UI-TARS-1.5) vs OpenAI CUA 23.4%.
Unified cross-platform action space: single model weights cover Windows/macOS/Linux/Android/web without per-platform fine-tuning.
MCP server exposure: UI-TARS-desktop wraps its operator as discoverable MCP tools, making it composable with any LLM orchestrator.
Inference-time scaling: UI-TARS-1.5 shows monotonic task success improvement with more interaction rounds, not diminishing returns.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Two-tier action dispatch: instant (grounding-only) vs deliberate (thought-chain) mode
portable_to_ghost.value: For known-pattern steps, call the grounding model with the GROUNDING prompt (no thought) for sub-500ms dispatch. Only escalate to full thought-chain VLM on failure, novel tasks, or low-confidence scores. Ghost already has fast DXGI capture; savings come from skipping reasoning tokens on routine clicks.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: A Rust enum ActionTier::Instant | ActionTier::Deliberate routes to different VLM system prompts in ghost-vlm. Deliberate tier appends last N (screenshot_hash, action, outcome) from a session ring buffer.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Adopt 0-1000 normalized coordinate convention as Ghost's canonical grounding protocol
portable_to_ghost.value: Resolution-agnostic, DPI-safe, and matches what Qwen-2-VL / UI-TARS / SeeClick already output natively. Eliminates per-model coordinate adapters. Scaling: px = round(coord * screen_dim / 1000).
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Add CoordNorm(u16, u16) to ghost-core. One fn norm_to_px(coord: u16, dim: u32) -> i32. Replaces ad-hoc coordinate parsing in current VLM response handlers.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Inference-time reflection via failure ring buffer injected into VLM prompt
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost cannot retrain with DPO, but can approximate reflection: maintain a per-session ring buffer of last 5 (obs_hash, action, success). On failure, prepend a negative hint to the next VLM prompt: 'Last attempt: click(423,318) -> element not found. Do not repeat.'
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: RingBuffer<StepRecord> in ghost-session. On execute() returning Err, the session appends the failure hint string to the next VLM system prompt. Bounded at 5 entries to stay within token budget.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Streaming event bus over MCP stdio to solve TTFT dead-wait UX
portable_to_ghost.value: UI-TARS-desktop's top UX complaint is TTFT latency with no feedback. Ghost's 137 MCP tools have the same problem. Emit ActionStarted, ScreenshotCaptured, VlmThinking, ActionParsed, ActionExecuted events as newline-delimited JSON before the final tool result.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: GhostEvent enum + tokio broadcast channel inside each tool handler. Emit via serde_json::to_writer before blocking execute(). MCP clients that support streaming content blocks render progress; others get the final result unchanged.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Route ghost_locate_by_description / ghost_find to a local UI-TARS-7B-DPO via vLLM
portable_to_ghost.value: UI-TARS-7B-DPO achieves 89.5% ScreenSpot vs 40-60% for generic VLMs. Sending grounding-only requests to a specialized local model is higher accuracy AND lower cost/latency than the current 90B cloud VLM for element location tasks.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: New VlmProvider variant UITarsGrounding { endpoint: Url } in ghost-vlm. Grounding tools call it with GROUNDING prompt template and parse 0-1000 coords. General reasoning tools keep Anthropic/NVIDIA provider.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Smart-resize screenshot preprocessing before VLM dispatch
portable_to_ghost.value: UI-TARS preprocesses screenshots to keep total pixels within [100*784, 16384*784] and divisible by 28 (ViT patch size). Ghost sends full DXGI captures. Adding smart_resize in Rust cuts image token count significantly, reducing cost and TTFT, while the tracked rescale ratio preserves coordinate accuracy.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: fn smart_resize(w: u32, h: u32) -> (u32, u32) in ghost-capture. Store orig+resized dims in ScreenshotMeta. Coord back-projection uses meta to convert 0-1000 to orig-resolution pixels.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Typed action parser crate replacing ad-hoc VLM response parsing
portable_to_ghost.value: UI-TARS's action_parser is Python regex. Ghost needs this in Rust. A ghost-action-parser crate parses VLM output into a typed Action enum (Click, Drag, Type, Hotkey, Scroll, Wait, Finished, CallUser) using nom or regex crate. Eliminates subprocess, makes dispatch synchronous and zero-copy.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: New crate ghost-action-parser in Ghost workspace. Consumed by ghost-mcp tool handlers: parse VLM response string into Action enum, then match arms dispatch to SendInput or session state. Prevents 'wrong format crashes loop' class of bugs.

pitfalls: Coordinate drift on DPI-scaled or multi-monitor setups: 0-1000 normalization assumes a flat single-screen coordinate space. Qwen-2-VL smart_resize introduces double-scaling - if you resize before sending, you must track resize ratio AND DPI scale factor separately. UI-TARS-desktop has open bugs on this (issue #563).
NutJS is not undetectable: nut.js goes through Node.js native bindings adding timing jitter and detectable patterns. Ghost's direct Rust SendInput is cleaner. Do not copy the NutJS approach.
Electron overhead: running a Chromium shell for the agent UI adds 200-400MB RAM and startup latency. Ghost as a Rust binary over MCP stdio avoids this entirely.

System-2 token cost: thought chain adds 100-300 extra tokens per step. At 25 steps that is 2500-7500 overhead tokens per task, plus screenshot tokens. Apply GROUNDING template aggressively for simple navigation.

Long-horizon planning ceiling: beyond 10-15 steps the 32k context budget is exhausted and old screenshots are silently dropped. No persistent cross-session memory. UI-TARS-2 acknowledges this as an open research problem.

Grounding data dependence: UI-TARS's benchmark dominance comes from 25M proprietary element-coordinate pairs. Reproducing this without ByteDance's data pipeline is not feasible. Ghost must call an existing grounding model rather than train from scratch.

VLM-only perception: when screenshots are ambiguous (overlapping windows, custom-rendered game UI, sub-pixel text), the model has no fallback. Ghost's UIA tree plus Windows.Media.Ocr layer is a meaningful advantage over UI-TARS - use it as a post-grounding verification pass.

Hard 25-iteration cap: MAX_LOOP is an arbitrary constant in the desktop app, not a model capability limit. Any task requiring more than 25 steps fails silently. Ghost should expose a configurable per-intent iteration budget.

research.what_it_is: Agent-TARS is ByteDance's open-source multimodal AI agent stack (Apache 2.0, ~33k GitHub stars as of May 2026) built on top of the UI-TARS vision-language model. It ships a TypeScript monorepo with a CLI (`@agent-tars/cli`, Node.js >= 22), a Web UI, and an Electron desktop app (UI-TARS Desktop), providing browser and desktop automation driven by screenshot-only VLM inference. The underlying model (UI-TARS, built on Qwen2-VL) is separately available in 7B and 72B parameter sizes and was open-weighted by ByteDance Seed.
research.architecture: Perception: at each agent step, a screenshot (1120x1120, dual-resolution encoder) is captured via the active operator (NutJS for desktop, Puppeteer-core for browser). The image is base64-encoded with a scaleFactor property and sent with the instruction and rolling conversation history to the VLM. Grounding: the VLM outputs action tokens in natural language, e.g. `click(start_box='<|box_start|>(423,611)<|box_end|>')`. The `@ui-tars/action-parser` package deserializes these into structured `PredictionParsed[]` objects, scaling the normalized 0-1000 coordinates back to actual screen pixels using the scaleFactor. Planning: the agent uses System-2 reasoning embedded in the model - it generates a thought before each action (task decomposition, reflection, milestone recognition, trial-and-error). There is no separate planner LLM; planning and grounding are unified in a single VLM forward pass. Action: the `Operator.execute()` method dispatches the parsed action. For desktop, NutJS synthesizes OS-level mouse/keyboard events. For browser, the DOM mode uses Puppeteer's accessibility-tree label index; visual grounding mode uses `browser_vision_screen_click` with pixel coordinates delivered via CDP through puppeteer-core. Verification: after each action, a new screenshot is captured; the VLM compares before/after implicitly in context and can emit a `wait()` or corrective action. The GUIAgent loop terminates on `finished(content='...')`, `MAX_LOOP`, or error. Event streaming: every step emits typed events via an `onData: (data: GUIAgentData) => void` callback covering states `INIT`, `RUNNING`, `END`, `MAX_LOOP`; Agent TARS CLI v0.3.0 added an Event Stream Viewer for replay and debugging.
research.grounding_approach: The VLM outputs normalized coordinates in a 1000x1000 space using special delimiter tokens: `click(start_box='<|box_start|>(x1,y1)<|box_end|>')` where x1 and y1 are integers from 0-1000 representing fractions of screen width/height. The `action-parser` package rescales these to real pixels using the screenshot's scaleFactor (device pixel ratio). There is NO a11y tree, no set-of-marks overlay, and no separate grounding model in the default visual mode - the single end-to-end VLM (Qwen2-VL fine-tuned on 7.5M grounding samples + 23.4M open-source GUI elements) predicts both reasoning and coordinates in one generation pass. For the DOM browser mode, no coordinates are used at all - Puppeteer's `browser_get_clickable_elements` returns label indexes and the agent clicks by index, bypassing VLM coordinate prediction entirely. The hybrid mode uses DOM-first; VLM coordinate prediction is the fallback when DOM fails.
research.action_space: Actions are serialized as function-call strings parsed from VLM text output. Full desktop action space: `click(start_box)`, `left_double(start_box)`, `right_single(start_box)`, `drag(start_box, end_box)`, `hotkey(key)`, `type(content)`, `scroll(start_box, direction)`, `wait()`, `finished(content)`. Mobile extras: `long_press`, `open_app`, `press_home`, `press_back`. Browser MCP tools (20+ via `@agent-infra/mcp-server-browser`): `browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_hover`, `browser_form_input_fill`, `browser_select`, `browser_press_key`, `browser_get_text`, `browser_get_markdown`, `browser_get_clickable_elements`, `browser_read_links`, `browser_screenshot`, `browser_vision_screen_capture`, `browser_vision_screen_click`, `browser_scroll`, `browser_evaluate`, `browser_tab_list`, `browser_new_tab`, `browser_switch_tab`, `browser_go_back`, `browser_go_forward`, `browser_close_tab`. MCP client supports four transports: in-memory, stdio, SSE, streamable HTTP. Total MCP tool ecosystem accessible to the agent: ~314 tools across all mounted servers.
speed_tactics: W4A8 quantization on UI-TARS-2 raises token throughput from 29.6 to 47 tokens/sec and cuts average interaction-round latency from 4.0s to 2.5s, at a cost of ~3pp accuracy on OSWorld (47.5 -> 44.4%).
Hybrid browser mode uses DOM-path (no VLM inference) as primary, reserving VLM for fallback - reducing expensive model calls on well-structured pages.
The agent-infra MCP client offers in-memory transport for tools in the same process, avoiding network hop latency.
GUIAgent callback streaming (onData) allows partial UI updates during model generation without waiting for full action completion.
Agent TARS CLI v0.3.0 added timing statistics per tool call, exposing where latency is spent for targeted optimization.
UI-TARS-2 uses asynchronous server-based RL rollouts with stateful environment preservation across tool invocations, reducing environment reset overhead during training (not inference).
No two-tier fast/slow model routing is described - every step goes through the full VLM. No documented speculative decoding or prefix caching in the inference stack.
standout_features: Full-stack open release under Apache 2.0: model weights, framework, Electron runtime, MCP infrastructure, and behavior-log store are all public - unprecedented for a production GUI agent.
Unified action space across desktop, browser, and mobile with a single model checkpoint; no platform-specific model variants needed.
Iterative data flywheel: hundreds of VMs run rollouts, traces are reflectively filtered and fed back via DPO; OSWorld jumped from 24.6% (Jan 2025) to 47.5% (Sep 2025) in 8 months.
Three-mode browser strategy (DOM / VLM / Hybrid) with CLI flag to select, giving developers explicit control over the speed-accuracy tradeoff.
Protocol-driven event stream with full replay/debugging via Event Stream Viewer - every screenshot, reasoning chain, and action is logged and replayable.
Remote Computer Operator and Remote Browser Operator added in v0.2.0: agent can control a headless browser or remote desktop over a REST API without co-location.
Model predicts coordinates in a 1000x1000 normalized space, making grounding resolution-independent and enabling cross-device consistency without retraining.
UI-TARS-2 hierarchical memory: working memory (recent steps verbatim) + episodic memory (semantic compression of older history), reducing context growth while preserving task continuity.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Adopt the 1000x1000 normalized coordinate convention as Ghost's internal coordinate system for VLM responses
portable_to_ghost.value: Any grounding model (NVIDIA llama-3.2-90b-vision, future ONNX models) can return coordinates in this space; Ghost scales once to real pixels using the DPI factor already captured by DXGI, making model swap-outs zero-friction.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Add a CoordSpace::Normalized1000 variant to Ghost's existing coordinate types; scale to physical pixels at the SendInput boundary using the monitor DPI already queried via GetDpiForMonitor. One 2-line multiply in the action dispatch path.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Implement a three-tier browser strategy: UIA-tree-first (fast, no VLM), VLM-coordinate fallback, hybrid with explicit per-call selector
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost already has UIA; using it as the DOM-equivalent primary path avoids a VLM round-trip on standard HTML controls, cutting latency by the full model inference time (~2-4s) for the common case.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's UIA walker already produces IUIAutomationElement handles. Add a BrowserStrategy enum {Uia, Vision, Hybrid} to ghost_click and related tools. In Hybrid mode, attempt UIA invoke; on failure or null element, fall through to vision coordinate click via SendInput.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Adopt the GUIAgent callback-streaming pattern: emit typed step events (PerceptionStart, ActionDispatched, VerificationComplete) over the MCP stdio channel
portable_to_ghost.value: Agent-TARS Event Stream Viewer showed that per-step observability dramatically reduces debugging time. Ghost clients (Claude Code, custom UIs) could render a live trace without polling.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: MCP tool responses already return JSON over stdio. Add a server-sent progress notification (MCP progress tokens) emitted at each loop phase. Serialize as {phase, screenshot_b64_thumb, action_str, elapsed_ms} matching Ghost's existing GhostEvent struct.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Implement a DOM-mode equivalent using Windows UI Automation snapshot + JSON serialization as context for the LLM, parallel to Agent-TARS DOM mode
portable_to_ghost.value: For browser windows controlled via edge/chrome, Ghost can walk UIA to extract the accessibility tree as a flat list of {name, role, bounding_rect} objects and pass it as structured context instead of a screenshot. This replaces an expensive VLM call with a cheap LLM text call for well-structured pages.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already calls UIA; serialize IUIAutomationElement trees to JSON using ghost_get_text. New MCP tool ghost_get_dom_snapshot returns the tree. The LLM picks an element by index; Ghost clicks the bounding_rect center via SendInput. Mirrors @agent-infra browser_get_clickable_elements + label-index click.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Implement hierarchical agent memory: verbatim working memory for last N steps, LLM-compressed episodic memory for older history
portable_to_ghost.value: UI-TARS-2's biggest reliability gain over v1 came from episodic memory preventing context explosion on long tasks. Ghost's multi-step browser tasks currently have unbounded context growth.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Store each step as a GhostStepRecord {screenshot_hash, action_str, observation_str} in a ring buffer (last 5 steps verbatim). After step 5, call a cheap Haiku-tier LLM to compress the oldest step into a one-sentence episodic summary. Inject verbatim + summaries into the next VLM prompt. Pure Rust ring buffer + async LLM call, no new dependencies.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Add W4A8 or GGUF-quantized UI-TARS-7B as a local ONNX/llama.cpp grounding model option for offline/low-latency operation
portable_to_ghost.value: Agent-TARS showed quantized inference at 47 tok/s with 2.5s round-trip. Ghost already has a multi-provider VLM client; a local ONNX endpoint would eliminate API latency and cost for high-frequency automation loops.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's VlmProvider trait accepts any HTTP endpoint returning action tokens. Point it at a local llama.cpp server running UI-TARS-7B-Q4. The normalized-1000 coordinate output format is identical to the API version - no parser changes needed. Trade-off: 7B accuracy vs 90B; use for bulk/repetitive tasks, escalate to API for complex reasoning.

pitfalls: The screenshot-only VLM approach is fundamentally slow: each step requires a full VLM forward pass at 1120x1120 resolution. At 4.0s per round unquantized, multi-step browser tasks take minutes. Do NOT copy this as the primary path for Ghost's latency-sensitive use cases.

The Puppeteer-based browser operator (DOM mode) clicks by Puppeteer label index, NOT by real OS input - this is detectable by anti-bot systems that monitor CDP injection vs real input events. Ghost's SendInput approach is architecturally superior for undetectability.
Hybrid mode's 'DOM first, VLM fallback' requires two sequential attempts when DOM fails, adding latency. A smarter signal (e.g., detect canvas/WebGL frames before choosing strategy) would be better.

The normalized 1000x1000 coordinate system means an off-by-one in scaling produces wrong clicks at high DPI. The scaleFactor from screenshot must be applied consistently at every coordinate use site - a single miss silently misfires.

Agent-TARS has no documented prompt caching or KV-cache reuse across steps. The full conversation history is re-encoded each step, making token costs O(N^2) in task length. Long tasks become expensive and slow.
The MCP client has no documented reconnection logic or connection pooling. For Ghost's 137-tool production use, this needs explicit hardening.

System-2 reasoning (think-before-act) adds ~30-50% more tokens per step vs a direct-action model. On tasks where the correct action is obvious, this is pure overhead. Agent-TARS has no fast path that skips the thought step.

Benchmark numbers (OSWorld 47.5%, AndroidWorld 73.3%) are for UI-TARS-2 (72B, Sep 2025). The 7B model scores are substantially lower (~18-20% OSWorld). Do not treat top-line numbers as representative of deployable local model performance.

research.what_it_is: Midscene.js is an open-source (MIT), TypeScript-first UI automation framework from ByteDance's Web Infra team (same group as Rspack). It drives web (Puppeteer/Playwright/Chrome Bridge), Android (adb), and iOS (WebDriverAgent) interfaces through VLM visual grounding on screenshots alone, with no mandatory DOM dependency for actions. As of mid-2026 the repo has ~13,500 GitHub stars and is in active production use inside ByteDance's e-commerce testing pipeline.
research.architecture: Perception: DXGI-equivalent is a platform driver (Puppeteer page.screenshot / adb screencap / WebDriverAgent) that captures a PNG; the optional screenshotShrinkFactor param downscales it before sending to the model (halving dimensions cuts tokens ~4x). Grounding: screenshot (base64) is sent to a VLM with a natural-language element description; the model outputs normalized [0,1] coordinates or bounding boxes; the @ui-tars/action-parser library parses the model response into {x, y, rect, center, dpr} and scales to device pixels. Planning: aiAct() sends the full task prompt to the Planning model, which returns a step sequence (Thought + Action chain); each step's element location can be re-routed to the Insight/Locate model. A merged Plan+Locate single-call path (default since v0.x) gives 30% faster response at some accuracy cost; deepLocate=true forces a two-call split for hard targets. Action: platform driver executes the concrete interaction (Playwright page.click(x,y), adb shell input tap, etc.). Verification: aiAssert() sends a new screenshot + assertion string to the Insight model, which returns a boolean + reasoning string; throws on false. Caching layer sits between planning and VLM: XPath (web only) and plan step strings are stored in .cache.yaml files; on replay the cached XPath is verified live; if the DOM node is gone, it falls back to VLM re-grounding. aiQuery/aiAssert results are never cached.
research.grounding_approach: Pure screenshot-only VLM grounding: no a11y tree, no set-of-marks annotation, no DOM sent for UI actions (DOM can optionally be appended for data extraction only via domIncluded option). The VLM (Qwen3-VL, Doubao-1.6-vision, UI-TARS, Gemini) receives the screenshot image + a natural-language element description and outputs normalized [0,1] coordinates representing the center of the target element's bounding box. The @ui-tars/action-parser library parses the raw model text output (format: click(start_box='(x,y)') or bbox JSON depending on model family) and multiplies by screen dimensions to get absolute pixel coordinates. UI-TARS specifically is trained to output normalized coordinates as text tokens (e.g. "0.42, 0.67"), achieving 94.2% on ScreenSpot-V2 and 61.6% on ScreenSpot-Pro (vs Claude 3.7 at 87.6%/27.7%). MIDSCENE_MODEL_FAMILY env var must be set correctly because coordinate format differs per model family. The deepLocate=true flag splits the call: first call does planning/identification, second call does precise coordinate regression on a cropped region, improving accuracy on small/ambiguous targets at 2x VLM cost.
research.action_space: Instant Actions (AI locates element, caller specifies verb - single VLM call): aiTap, aiHover, aiInput (modes: replace/typeOnly/clear), aiClearInput, aiKeyboardPress, aiScroll (scrollType/direction/distance), aiPinch (in/out, distance, duration), aiLongPress (duration), aiDoubleClick, aiRightClick. Each instant action accepts: locate (string description), deepLocate (bool), cacheable (bool), xpath (string, bypasses VLM if provided). Planning Actions (VLM plans multi-step sequence): aiAct(prompt, {cacheable, deepThink, deepLocate, fileChooserAccept, abortSignal}) - returns output string if task produces one. Data Extraction: aiQuery<T>(dataDemand) where dataDemand is a typed schema description like '{name: string, age: number}[]'; aiBoolean, aiNumber, aiString for scalar extraction. Assertion: aiAssert(string, errorMsg?) - throws with VLM reasoning on failure. aiWaitFor(assertion, {timeoutMs, checkIntervalMs}) - polls aiAssert. Utility: aiLocate(describe) returns {rect, center, dpr}; freezePageContext()/unfreezePageContext() snapshot the page for batch queries without re-capture; runYaml(string) executes YAML flow. YAML action verbs: ai (planning), aiAssert, sleep, plus env var interpolation via ${VAR}.
speed_tactics: Instant action tier: aiTap/aiInput/etc bypass planning model entirely - single VLM locate call vs multi-step planning loop of aiAct
Merged Plan+Locate prompt: single VLM call handles both task decomposition and element coordinate output, 30% faster than split calls (default path)
deepLocate=false by default: one VLM call per action; deepLocate=true reserved for ambiguous elements (costs 2x)
XPath cache: web-only, stores located element XPath keyed by natural-language description in .cache.yaml; replayed scripts verify XPath liveness then skip VLM entirely
Plan step cache: aiAct results cached by prompt string; read-write/read-only/write-only strategies configurable per agent
screenshotShrinkFactor: shrinks image dimensions before VLM call (factor 2 = 4x area reduction = ~4x fewer image tokens); default 1, mobile can use 2
freezePageContext: reuse a single screenshot capture across multiple aiQuery/aiLocate calls, eliminating redundant screen captures
Intent-based model routing: cheap fast VLM for grounding (Qwen3-VL), expensive LLM (GPT-5.1) only for planning steps when needed
DOM skip: pure-vision cuts DOM serialization cost, reducing token overhead by ~80% vs DOM-annotated approaches
replanningCycleLimit=20 default caps runaway planning loops
standout_features: Instant action API tier cleanly separates 'I know what to do, just find it' (aiTap) from 'figure out the steps for me' (aiAct) - the most actionable DX insight for Ghost

Two-model routing: MIDSCENE_PLANNING_MODEL_* for step decomposition (can be a strong LLM like GPT-5.1), MIDSCENE_INSIGHT_MODEL_* for VQA/assertions, default model for grounding/locate - each role uses the optimal model
Typed data extraction: aiQuery<T> takes a TypeScript-style schema description string and returns structured JSON - zero schema definition ceremony
deepLocate two-pass: first call identifies, second call precision-crops and re-grounds on the sub-region; accuracy improves for small/icon targets
XPath cache with live validation: cached XPath is re-checked against live DOM; if text content changed, falls back to VLM automatically - no stale cache failures
freezePageContext: snapshot once, query many times - critical for batch assertion/extraction without redundant screen grabs
YAML declarative flows with JS interop: runYaml() callable from JS, env var interpolation, sequential task blocks - enables non-engineer test authoring
ScreenSpot-Pro 61.6% (UI-TARS-1.5) vs next best 27.7% - the grounding model quality gap is massive and directly drives reliability
Report merging tool: multiple agent runs merged into a single HTML replay with screenshots - built-in observability
screenshotShrinkFactor per-agent: token budget tunable at agent construction time, not globally

portable_to_ghost.idea: Instant vs Planning action tier in Ghost MCP API
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost's 137 MCP tools are mostly atomic. Add a ghost_act(prompt) planning tool that calls the VLM to decompose a multi-step task into a sequence of existing Ghost atomic tools (ghost_click, ghost_type, etc.), then executes them. Separately, keep ghost_click_by_description as the 'instant action' path: single VLM locate call, no planning overhead. This gives callers explicit control over cost/latency tradeoff.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: ghost_execute_intent already exists as a planning entry point; the split can be formalized by adding a ghost_act wrapper that calls the VLM planner and dispatches to existing primitives. The Rust side just needs a plan-executor loop over the existing action enum.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Two-model routing: separate locate model from planning model
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost already has a multi-provider VLM client. Route describe/locate calls (ghost_find, ghost_locate_by_description) to a fast cheap vision model (e.g. local UI-TARS-7B via ONNX or Qwen-VL API) and reserve the expensive model (NVIDIA llama-3.2-90b-vision / Anthropic) only for planning and assertion. This directly cuts per-action VLM cost on repetitive automation.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's VLM client in Rust can be extended with an intent enum (Locate, Plan, Assert) that selects provider at call time. Config drives which provider handles each intent.

portable_to_ghost.idea: deepLocate two-pass for small/icon targets
portable_to_ghost.value: When ghost_find or ghost_locate_by_description fails or returns low confidence, do a second VLM call on a cropped region around the best candidate bbox. Ghost already captures DXGI frames; cropping a sub-rect and re-encoding to PNG is trivial in Rust with the image crate. Pass the crop to the VLM with 'find the exact center of [element]' - precision improves dramatically for toolbar icons and small buttons.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Add deepLocate: bool to ghost_find/ghost_click_by_description. Rust crops the DXGI frame to the candidate bbox, encodes PNG, calls VLM again. Return center coordinates from second pass.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Normalized-coordinate output contract with VLM
portable_to_ghost.value: UI-TARS and Qwen-VL output normalized [0,1] coordinates as text tokens. Ghost should standardize on this: send screenshot + element description, parse model output for normalized (x,y), multiply by captured frame dimensions. This is resolution-agnostic and works correctly across DPI scaling. Currently Ghost may be doing ad-hoc coordinate parsing per model - unify it through a single CoordinateParser trait in Rust.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Define a GroundingResult { center_norm: (f32, f32), bbox_norm: Option<Rect>, confidence: Option<f32> } type. Each VLM adapter implements a parse_grounding_response() method. Scale to pixels using DXGI frame dimensions * DPI factor from Windows.Graphics.Display.

portable_to_ghost.idea: XPath/UIA-path cache with live validation
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost has Windows UI Automation tree access. After a successful ghost_find resolves to a UIA element, cache the UIA AutomationId + ClassName + Name tuple (analogous to XPath) keyed by the natural-language description. On next call, try to re-find the UIA element by cached path first; if it still exists and Name matches, skip VLM entirely. If not found or Name changed, fall back to VLM re-grounding. Store in a per-session in-memory HashMap<String, UiaElementPath> in Rust.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already uses UIA tree for ghost_find_text_local and related tools. The cache lives in a Mutex<HashMap> inside the Ghost state struct. The UIA path struct maps to IUIAutomationElement's cached properties. Web-only limitation in Midscene (XPath) becomes cross-app in Ghost (UIA path).

portable_to_ghost.idea: Page/screen context freeze for batch queries
portable_to_ghost.value: Add ghost_freeze_screen() / ghost_unfreeze_screen() that hold a single DXGI frame snapshot and route all subsequent VLM calls to that frozen frame. Critical for asserting multiple conditions on a single UI state without the screen changing between grabs. In Rust, store Arc<DxgiFrame> in Ghost state; all VLM calls read from the frozen frame instead of calling CaptureScreen().
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's DXGI capture is already centralized. Add an Option<Arc<CapturedFrame>> field to GhostState. ghost_freeze_screen captures and stores it; ghost_unfreeze_screen sets it to None. All VLM dispatch checks this field first.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Typed structured data extraction (ghost_query)
portable_to_ghost.value: Add ghost_query(schema_description: string) that sends the current screenshot + DOM/OCR text + a schema description to the Insight model and returns structured JSON. Directly mirrors aiQuery<T>. Ghost's Windows.Media.Ocr already extracts visible text; this can be bundled into the prompt alongside the screenshot to give the model richer signal for data extraction without full DOM.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: New MCP tool ghost_query takes a JSON schema description string. Ghost bundles screenshot + OCR text into the prompt. VLM returns JSON string. Ghost validates against the schema using a Rust JSON schema validator (jsonschema crate) and returns it. Replaces ad-hoc 'get text' + manual parsing in caller code.

portable_to_ghost.idea: YAML declarative script runner
portable_to_ghost.value: Expose ghost_run_yaml(yaml: string) MCP tool that parses a simple YAML flow (verbs: click, type, assert, wait, query, key) and dispatches to Ghost's existing MCP tools. Enables non-Rust test authors to write automation scripts without JavaScript or Rust. The Rust side uses serde_yaml to parse a Vec<YamlStep> and matches on verb to call internal action handlers.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: serde_yaml is already a common Rust dep. Define a YamlStep enum matching Ghost's existing action space. ghost_run_yaml loops over steps, dispatches, collects results. Supports ${ENV_VAR} substitution via the shellexpand crate.

pitfalls: Pure-vision is worse than hybrid on data-extraction tasks: aiQuery still optionally includes DOM for invisible attributes (image src, aria-label, off-screen text) - Ghost's OCR-only approach will miss these. Need to combine OCR + UIA tree properties for equivalent coverage.

XPath cache is web-only in Midscene - the analog for native desktop (UIA path) is more brittle because UIA AutomationIds are often missing or non-unique in Win32/WinForms apps. Do not assume the cache will hit reliably on legacy desktop apps.

screenshotShrinkFactor >3 causes coordinate regression errors: small icons become unlocatable. Ghost's DXGI captures are already high-fidelity; do not aggressively shrink for desktop grounding where pixel-perfect icon hits matter.
Caching does NOT cover aiQuery/aiAssert results - these always call the VLM. Do not plan a 'cache everything' strategy that assumes assertions are free on replay.
deepLocate two-pass doubles VLM cost and adds 1-2s latency - should be opt-in only, not the default path, or the fast-action advantage disappears.

Model family mismatch silently breaks coordinate parsing: each VLM outputs coordinates in a different text format. If MIDSCENE_MODEL_FAMILY is wrong, coordinates are silently mis-parsed. Ghost must implement per-model response parsers, not a single regex.

aiAct planning loop has a default limit of 20 replanning cycles - poorly specified tasks can exhaust the limit and terminate mid-task with no useful error. Ghost needs explicit loop budget + partial-result reporting.
Pure-vision on canvas/game surfaces works for clicking but fails for structured data extraction - no text layer. Ghost's OCR can help but will struggle with rotated/stylized text in game UIs.
ByteDance's own 85% test-authoring time reduction figure is unverified third-party marketing data - treat as directional, not a benchmark.

UI-TARS ScreenSpot-Pro 61.6% is impressive but still means ~38% of professional-grade UI elements are mis-located - reliability on complex enterprise desktop UIs will be substantially lower than on simple web pages.

research.target: Alibaba Mobile-Agent family (v3, v3.5) + GUI-Owl / GUI-Owl-1.5 grounding model (X-PLUG / Tongyi Lab)
research.what_it_is: Mobile-Agent-v3 is a four-agent (Manager, Worker, Reflector, Notetaker) GUI automation framework built on GUI-Owl, a Qwen2.5-VL-based foundation model post-trained on large-scale GUI interaction data. Released August 2025 by Alibaba's Tongyi Lab / X-PLUG group, it supports Android, HarmonyOS, Windows, macOS, Ubuntu, and browser targets through platform-specific controller abstractions. The successor, Mobile-Agent-v3.5 / GUI-Owl-1.5 (February 2026), upgrades the backbone to Qwen3-VL and ships 2B/4B/8B/32B/235B variants with instruct and thinking heads, achieving SOTA on 20+ GUI benchmarks.
research.architecture: Perception: DXGI-style screenshot (or ADB/HDC screen capture on mobile) is fed as a raw image to the GUI-Owl VLM; no a11y tree is used at inference time (a11y trees are used only during training data generation to label bounding boxes). Grounding: GUI-Owl predicts coordinates in the 0-1000 normalized range for mobile and absolute pixel values for desktop/PC (Qwen2.5-VL native format); a two-step crop-refinement pass is available: predict coarse location on full screenshot, crop and zoom the region, re-predict at higher resolution, then remap back. Planning: Manager agent receives the user task + RAG-retrieved app knowledge, decomposes into an ordered subgoal list, and dynamically updates the plan on each iteration. Action: Worker agent receives current screenshot, subgoal, prior Reflector feedback, and accumulated Notetaker notes; outputs a structured action tuple (action_type, coords, text). Verification: Reflector agent compares before/after screenshots and classifies the transition as SUCCESS or FAILURE with a diagnostic message. Memory: Notetaker agent runs only on SUCCESS steps; extracts salient screen information (credentials, addresses, codes) and appends to a running important_notes string in the shared InfoPool. InfoPool is a simple Python object holding the notes string plus current subgoal list, passed as context to each agent call. The execution loop in run_mobileagentv3.py runs: Manager -> Worker -> execute action -> Reflector -> (if SUCCESS) Notetaker -> Manager update, repeating until task done or max steps reached. Training: three phases - (1) supervised pre-training on grounding + planning + action-semantics data, (2) iterative tuning via self-evolving trajectory pipeline with step-level and trajectory-level critics, (3) online RL with TRPO/MRPO. Self-evolving pipeline: build virtual environment DAGs, roll out GUI-Owl, judge correctness at step and trajectory levels, generate hint-guided guidance for hard cases, retrain, repeat.
research.grounding_approach: GUI-Owl (Qwen2.5-VL base, 7B/32B): outputs absolute pixel coordinates in function-call action tuples directly from the screenshot, no a11y tree at inference. Mobile coordinate outputs are in 0-1000 normalized space then rescaled to device resolution; desktop outputs are raw pixel values matching Qwen2.5-VL's post-training coordinate convention. Training grounding data is sourced from a11y trees (bounding boxes + functional descriptions) augmented by MLLM-generated appearance/layout descriptions. For high-density PC UIs, SAM segmentation runs first to isolate dense regions, then the VLM grounds within each segment. Fine-grained text targets use OCR to get spatial positions before passing to VLM. Benchmark numbers: GUI-Owl-7B 92.8% ScreenSpot-V2, 54.9% ScreenSpot-Pro; GUI-Owl-32B 93.2% ScreenSpot-V2, 58.0% ScreenSpot-Pro; GUI-Owl-1.5-32B (Qwen3-VL) 72.9% ScreenSpot-Pro base, 80.3% with two-step crop refinement (SOTA among open-source as of February 2026). The crop-refinement pipeline: step 1 - predict coarse click on full screenshot; step 2 - crop a window around prediction, resize to high-res, re-predict; remap step 2 coordinates back to original resolution; discard step 1 prediction. AQuaUI (conditional quadtree) reduces visual tokens 29.5% at inference with <1% accuracy drop by exploiting temporal redundancy between consecutive screenshots.
research.action_space: Mobile (Android/HarmonyOS via ADB/HDC): tap(x,y), swipe(x1,y1,x2,y2,duration), type(text), key(keycode), long_press(x,y), answer(text), system_button(button), open(app), wait(), terminate(). Desktop (OSWorld): left_click(x,y), right_click(x,y), double_click(x,y), drag(x1,y1,x2,y2), scroll(x,y,direction,amount), mouse_move(x,y), hotkey(keys), type(text), key(keycode), wait(), terminate(). GUI-Owl-1.5 extends with: external tool_call(tool_name, args) and MCP-based API invocations as first-class action types, enabling hybrid GUI+API task execution (scored 47.6 on OSWorld-MCP benchmark). Browser actions go through Playwright with a CSS semantic object model (SOM) flag that annotates interactive elements with CSS selectors as an optional perception enhancement.
speed_tactics: No published wall-clock latency numbers for any version - a meaningful gap vs. Ghost's real benchmark.

AQuaUI conditional quadtree: reuses prior-frame quadtree as reference to prune static screen regions, cutting visual tokens 29.52% and inference time 13.22% on GUI-Owl-1.5-32B with 99.06% accuracy retention - purely at inference time, no retraining.

Two-tier action head design in GUI-Owl-1.5: lightweight Instruct head for low-latency high-throughput steps, deeper Thinking head (CoT transformer decoder) only invoked when multi-step reasoning is needed.
Model size ladder (2B to 235B) enables cloud-edge deployment: 2B/4B run locally on device, 32B+ on cloud.
vLLM deployment config documented: A100 96GB, max-model-len 32768, tensor-parallel-size 1 for 7B, with pixel budget capped at min_pixels=3136 / max_pixels=10035200 to control per-image token count.
MRPO online rollout buffer: oversample kn trajectories, subsample n for training groups, avoiding GRPO collapse - improves training efficiency not inference speed.
standout_features: Single unified policy across all device types (mobile, desktop, browser) via device-conditioned MRPO training - no per-platform model specialization needed.
Self-evolving trajectory pipeline with DAG-based task synthesis, two-tier correctness critics (step-level + trajectory-level), and iterative online RL creating a data flywheel without human labeling.
Two-step crop-refinement grounding achieving 80.3% ScreenSpot-Pro - a post-hoc inference technique requiring no model retraining, just two forward passes.
TRPO/MRPO: trajectory-level advantage normalization distributing sparse reward across all steps - solves the long-horizon sparse-reward problem for GUI agents specifically.
Native MCP tool-calling as a first-class action type (OSWorld-MCP score 47.6, beating Claude-4-Sonnet 43.3) - the model can decide between GUI actions and API calls within the same policy.

GUI-Critic-R1 (NeurIPS 2025): a separate critic model for pre-operative error diagnosis that predicts likely failure before executing an action - an offline safety net distinct from the Reflector's post-hoc correction.
GUI-Owl-1.5 MemGUI-Bench score 27.1 vs. UI-TARS-1.5-7B 8.3 - demonstrates genuine long-horizon cross-session memory capability, not just within-episode note-taking.
Thinking head with explicit chain-of-thought: can be toggled per step, enabling a fast/slow system-2 style inference within the same model weights.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Two-step crop-refinement grounding loop
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost already captures screenshots via DXGI. Adding a second VLM call on a zoomed crop of the predicted click region could push grounding accuracy from current baseline toward 80%+ on high-density UIs (CAD tools, IDE editors, dense web apps) - the exact scenario where Ghost's current UIA fallback is weakest.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's Rust VLM client already sends screenshots to NVIDIA/Anthropic endpoint. Add a crop_and_refine() function: after first coordinate prediction, extract a 400x400 px sub-image around predicted point using DXGI buffer slice, send as second request with same prompt, remap coordinates. No model changes needed.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Reflector agent as a post-action verification layer
portable_to_ghost.value: Mobile-Agent-v3's Reflector compares before/after screenshots and returns SUCCESS/FAILURE + diagnostic. Ghost currently has ghost_click_and_wait_for_text but no generic visual diff verification. A Reflector call after every action would catch misclicks silently and enable automatic retry without LLM re-planning the entire task.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already captures frames via DXGI. Add a GhostReflector struct that holds pre-action screenshot hash + intent string, captures post-action screenshot, sends both to VLM with a structured prompt ('Did this action achieve: {intent}? Answer SUCCESS or FAILURE with reason'), and returns a typed enum. Integrates with existing ghost_batch_actions retry logic.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Notetaker-style persistent InfoPool across action steps
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost tools are stateless across MCP calls. A lightweight InfoPool (a shared Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String,String>>> in Ghost's Rust server) populated by a Notetaker prompt on each successful step would let long-horizon tasks (fill multi-page form, scrape across tabs) carry forward extracted values (order IDs, prices, credentials) without re-querying the screen.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's MCP stdio server already maintains process state. Add ghost_note_set / ghost_note_get tools backed by an in-process HashMap. On successful step, call VLM with Notetaker prompt ('Extract any important values from this screen given task context') and store result. Expose via MCP so Claude can read/write notes.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Two-tier fast/slow action head routing (Instruct vs. Thinking)
portable_to_ghost.value: GUI-Owl-1.5's instruct head handles simple clicks fast; the thinking head engages for complex multi-step planning. Ghost could implement a heuristic router: simple actions (click button, type text, scroll) use a lightweight local model (ONNX CLIP-based grounding) or the smaller NVIDIA endpoint; complex planning steps (multi-app workflow, error recovery) escalate to the full 90B VLM. This mirrors Ghost's existing multi-provider VLM client.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's VLM provider abstraction already supports multiple endpoints. Add an ActionComplexityClassifier (rule-based heuristic: if action_count == 1 and element_type in [Button, Link, Input] → fast; else → slow). Route fast actions to a local Qwen2.5-VL-7B ONNX or smaller API model. Reduces per-action latency for 80% of steps.

portable_to_ghost.idea: AQuaUI-style visual token budgeting between consecutive frames
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost takes full DXGI screenshots every action step. Between steps where the UI is mostly static (typing into a field, scrolling), most of the visual context is unchanged. A quadtree diff on consecutive DXGI bitmaps could identify changed regions and crop the VLM input to only include changed areas + a thumbnail of the full screen, cutting token cost 20-30% on steady-state workflows.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's DXGI capture returns raw BGRA bitmaps. Implement a quadtree frame differ in Rust (compare pixel blocks recursively, identify changed quads above a threshold). Compose a minimal VLM context: changed region crops + 256x256 thumbnail. This is pure Rust image processing, no ML needed.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Self-evolving trajectory data collection for Ghost grounding fine-tuning
portable_to_ghost.value: The self-evolving pipeline (virtual env rollout -> step-level critic -> retrain) is the source of GUI-Owl's benchmark edge. Ghost cannot train a VLM, but it CAN generate labeled grounding data on Windows apps by running tasks, capturing screenshots, recording UIA-confirmed click targets, and storing (screenshot, query, correct_pixel_coord) triples. This dataset could be used to fine-tune a local Qwen2.5-VL-7B or to build a CLIP-style embedding index for nearest-neighbor grounding.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already knows the UIA element that was clicked (it performs the click via SendInput and can query UIA for the bounding rect of the focused element post-click). Add a GhostTrajectoryRecorder that stores (screenshot_path, intent_text, uia_confirmed_bbox) on each successful ghost_click call. After N sessions, export as a ScreenSpot-compatible grounding dataset for external fine-tuning.

portable_to_ghost.idea: GUI-Actor-style attention verifier as a plug-in grounding confidence filter
portable_to_ghost.value: GUI-Actor's verifier selects among multiple candidate regions without needing the full model backbone retrained. Ghost could call a lightweight verifier model (GUI-Actor-3B weights are MIT licensed) after its primary VLM predicts a click coordinate, to confirm the predicted point actually overlaps the intended element before executing SendInput. This catches hallucinated coordinates on dense UIs.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost's VLM client can call a second, smaller endpoint (GUI-Actor-3B via local vLLM or ONNX) with the predicted coordinate rendered as an overlaid cursor on the screenshot. The verifier returns a confidence score; below threshold, Ghost falls back to UIA tree search. GUI-Actor weights are MIT licensed (microsoft/GUI-Actor-3B-Qwen2.5-VL on HuggingFace).

pitfalls: No published latency benchmarks anywhere in the paper, repo, or model cards - all claims are accuracy-only. Ghost should not assume acceptable per-step latency without benchmarking vLLM-served GUI-Owl-7B locally.

The 0-1000 normalized coordinate scheme for mobile introduces precision loss at low resolutions and edge cases near boundaries - Ghost targets fixed Windows screen resolutions where absolute pixel output (Qwen2.5-VL native) is strictly better.

The SAM segmentation step for high-density UIs adds a third model call to the grounding pipeline - expensive and brittle if SAM segments incorrectly. Ghost's UIA tree is a superior fallback for dense element identification without extra model calls.

Self-evolving pipeline requires virtual environment infrastructure (Android emulators, Ubuntu VMs) and a critic model per platform - not reproducible on Windows-only Ghost without significant infra work.

GUI-Owl-1.5's 32B instruct model requires A100-class GPU for acceptable throughput; the 7B/8B models are practical for local deployment but score ~15 points lower on ScreenSpot-Pro (no crop refinement: ~54.9% vs. 80.3%). Ghost's cloud VLM path (NVIDIA 90B) is likely competitive without running GUI-Owl locally.
The crop-refinement trick's two VLM calls double latency on every grounding step - the 80.3% number is not achievable at the same speed as the base model's 72.9%.
MCP tool-calling integration (OSWorld-MCP) is evaluated in a controlled benchmark environment; real-world reliability of mixed GUI+API workflows in production Windows apps is unverified.
InfoPool is just a string accumulated by a prompted VLM - there is no structured schema, deduplication, or eviction policy. In long tasks this degrades (noisy notes, growing context, stale values).

The four-agent loop (Manager + Worker + Reflector + Notetaker) makes 3-4 VLM calls per step. At 1-2s per VLM call that is 3-8 seconds per action step, which is unacceptable for interactive desktop automation where users expect sub-second response.

research.target: Microsoft OmniParser v1/v2 (pure-vision screen parsing, github.com/microsoft/OmniParser)
research.what_it_is: OmniParser is a two-stage pure-vision UI screen parsing pipeline built by Microsoft Research that converts raw screenshots into structured, LLM-consumable element lists without any accessibility tree. It consists of a fine-tuned YOLOv8 Nano interactable-region detector and a fine-tuned Florence-2 (v2) / BLIP-v2 (v1) caption model, fused with an OCR stage (EasyOCR or PaddleOCR), producing annotated screenshots with numeric bounding-box IDs alongside a structured JSON element list. The system is model-agnostic - the parsed output is fed to any VLM (GPT-4o, Qwen2.5-VL, DeepSeek R1, Anthropic, etc.) which then selects element IDs rather than predicting raw coordinates.
research.architecture:
**Perception:** DXGI-equivalent is a PIL/numpy screenshot ingestion; image is passed as-is to both YOLO and OCR in parallel.

**Stage 1 - YOLO detection:** `predict_yolo()` calls `model.predict(source=image, conf=BOX_THRESHOLD, iou=0.1)` on the raw PIL image. YOLOv8 Nano outputs pixel-space `xyxy` bounding boxes + confidence scores. These are immediately normalized to [0,1] by dividing by `[w, h, w, h]`. Each box is tagged `{'type': 'icon', 'bbox': [x1,y1,x2,y2], 'interactivity': True, 'content': None}`.

**Stage 2 - OCR (parallel):** `check_ocr_box()` runs EasyOCR (default) or PaddleOCR on the full image. Output is `(text_strings[], xyxy_pixel_boxes[])`. Each OCR result is normalized and tagged `{'type': 'text', 'bbox': ..., 'interactivity': False, 'content': str, 'source': 'box_ocr_content_ocr'}`.

**Stage 3 - Overlap removal:** `remove_overlap_new()` implements a custom IoU filter with two asymmetric rules: (a) between YOLO boxes, keep the smaller one when IoU > threshold; (b) between YOLO boxes and OCR boxes, if an OCR box is inside an icon box (>80% containment), absorb the OCR text as the icon's label and drop the OCR box; if the icon is inside an OCR box, drop the icon. This produces a deduplicated merged list.

**Stage 4 - Caption generation:** All icon boxes with `content=None` (those not resolved by OCR text) are cropped to 64x64 px and batched through Florence-2 (prompt: `"<CAPTION>"`) or BLIP-v2 in batches of 128. Florence-2 runs `max_new_tokens=20, num_beams=1, do_sample=False` - fast greedy decode. Each crop gets a short functional description ("close button", "search icon", etc.).

**Stage 5 - Set-of-Marks annotation:** All boxes are converted from xyxy to cxcywh, then pixel-scaled via `boxes * [w, h, w, h]`. A `BoxAnnotator` draws colored rectangles + sequential numeric IDs on a copy of the source frame. Label placement uses an algorithm that minimizes overlap between numeric labels and other boxes. The annotated image (base64 PNG) + label_coordinates dict (id -> normalized [cx,cy,w,h]) are returned alongside the element list.

**Output to VLM:** The annotated screenshot + a text list like `"Text Box ID 0: File", "Icon Box ID 3: gear/settings icon"` are sent to the VLM. The VLM responds with the ID of the element to interact with, NOT raw coordinates. The calling code then maps the ID back to pixel coordinates using `label_coordinates`.

**Grounding:** VLM selects an integer ID -> system looks up `label_coordinates[id]` -> converts normalized cxcywh back to pixel xyxy -> computes click point as center of box.
research.grounding_approach: Two-step indirect grounding via Set-of-Marks (SoM): the VLM never outputs coordinates directly. Instead, OmniParser overlays sequential integer IDs (0, 1, 2, ...) as colored bounding-box annotations on the screenshot, then passes both the annotated image AND a text manifest (`"Icon Box ID N: description"`) to the VLM in the same prompt. The VLM outputs a bare integer (the box ID). The pipeline resolves that ID to normalized center-XY + WH from a pre-built `label_coordinates` dict, then scales to pixel space. Coordinate format throughout the pipeline is normalized float [0,1] in cxcywh; final click = `(cx * img_w, cy * img_h)`. The YOLO model itself outputs raw pixel xyxy which is immediately divided by [w,h,w,h]. No coordinate regression from the VLM; no a11y tree; no DOM. The detection backbone (YOLOv8 Nano) is a pure vision model trained on 67k web screenshots with DOM-derived ground-truth boxes - it has no text understanding, only spatial pattern recognition for "looks interactive".
research.action_space: OmniParser itself is a perception/grounding layer only - it does not execute actions. Its output is: (1) base64-encoded annotated PNG with numeric overlays, (2) a `label_coordinates` dict mapping integer IDs to normalized [cx,cy,w,h], (3) a list of element dicts `{'type':'icon'|'text', 'bbox':[x1,y1,x2,y2], 'interactivity':bool, 'content':str, 'source':str}`. Downstream agents (OmniTool) use this to drive: click (center of bbox), type, hotkey, scroll. OmniTool wraps a Windows 11 VM via Docker and sends actions through standard desktop automation.
speed_tactics: V2 reduced Florence-2 caption input image size (crop resized to 64x64 before caption model), cutting latency by 60% vs v1 (0.6s/frame on A100, 0.8s on RTX 4090)
Florence-2 caption batched at 128 icons per forward pass to maximize GPU utilization
OCR and YOLO run sequentially in the reference code but are conceptually parallelizable; overlap removal is pure CPU Python O(N^2) over N boxes - fast because N is typically <100
YOLOv8 Nano is the smallest YOLO variant (~6MB when exported to OpenVINO IR), enabling CPU-only inference on Intel Core i9 at usable speed
ONNX export available: onnx-community/OmniParser-icon_detect on HuggingFace; OpenVINO IR export is documented (input shape 1x3x640x640 BCHW, output ~6.1MB model)
No caching, no tiling, no instant-action tier - every frame is a fresh full inference pass
EasyOCR is the default OCR (GPU-accelerated); PaddleOCR available as alternative with use_dilation=True, det_db_score_mode='slow' for higher accuracy at cost of speed
standout_features: Pure vision - zero dependency on a11y tree, DOM, or OS APIs; works on any screen including games, VMs, remote desktops, and locked-down apps where UIA is unavailable or returns nothing
Model-agnostic VLM backend: the annotated screenshot + element list can be fed to any vision-capable LLM (GPT-4o, Qwen2.5-VL, DeepSeek, Anthropic, Llama-3.2-V) without code changes
SoM approach makes coordinate grounding robust: VLM selects an integer ID rather than regressing float coordinates, eliminating off-by-one pixel failures and hallucinated coordinates
Interactability classification (v1.5+): the YOLO model predicts whether each detected element is actually interactive, filtering purely decorative elements before sending to VLM
Icon semantic captioning: Florence-2 generates functional descriptions ('minimize window button', 'search field') so VLM can match intent to element even when icon has no text label
39.6% on ScreenSpot Pro (vs 0.8% GPT-4o alone, 18.9% Anthropic Computer Use) - largest published accuracy gain from pure preprocessing on this benchmark
OCR-icon fusion with asymmetric containment logic: OCR text inside an icon box is absorbed as the icon's label rather than creating two overlapping elements
ONNX/OpenVINO export of the YOLO detection head (~6MB) enables CPU-only grounding on commodity hardware without a GPU
Training data derived from DOM trees of real web pages: 67k screenshots with ground-truth interactive bounding boxes, giving the detector real-world web app coverage

portable_to_ghost.idea: ONNX YOLO icon detector as a Ghost grounding primitive
portable_to_ghost.value: Run the 6MB OmniParser-icon_detect ONNX model locally via ort (Rust ort crate, ONNX Runtime bindings) on every DXGI frame. Input: 1x3x640x640 BCHW float32 tensor from DXGI capture. Output: xyxy boxes + confidence. This gives Ghost a no-GPU, no-API icon detection pass that runs in ~50ms on CPU, complementing UIA for apps where UIA returns empty trees (games, Electron with accessibility off, RDP sessions).
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: ort crate already exists in Rust ecosystem; DXGI frame is already captured as raw bytes; add a preprocessing step (resize to 640x640, normalize to [0,1], BCHW layout) and a NMS postprocessing step. Output boxes in Ghost's native pixel-space Rect type. No Python dependency.

portable_to_ghost.idea: SoM overlay + VLM ID selection as a fallback grounding path
portable_to_ghost.value: When UIA grounding fails or returns no actionable element for a target description, Ghost can fall back to: (1) draw sequential integer IDs over all detected elements on the current DXGI frame, (2) send annotated PNG + element manifest to Ghost's existing multi-provider VLM client, (3) parse the integer ID from VLM response, (4) map ID back to pixel center for SendInput click. This mirrors exactly what OmniParser does and Ghost already has all the infrastructure (DXGI, VLM client, SendInput).
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already has a VLM client (NVIDIA llama-3.2-90b-vision / Anthropic) and DXGI capture. Add a SoM annotation step in Rust using imageproc or similar to draw numbered rectangles. The VLM prompt template is a trivial addition. Total new code: ~200 lines Rust.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Windows.Media.Ocr + YOLO fusion with OmniParser's asymmetric containment overlap removal
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost already has Windows.Media.Ocr (on-device, fast). Add OmniParser's exact `remove_overlap_new` logic in Rust: merge OCR word boxes with YOLO icon boxes using the two containment rules (OCR-inside-icon absorbs text as icon label; icon-inside-OCR drops the icon). This produces a richer element list than either source alone, identical in semantics to OmniParser's output but using Ghost's native OCR instead of EasyOCR/PaddleOCR.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Pure algorithmic port - no ML dependency. The IoU + containment math is ~60 lines. Ghost's existing OCR output is already in pixel-space xyxy. Normalize to [0,1] before fusion, scale back to pixels afterward. Output: Vec<UiElement> with type/bbox/content/source fields.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Offline Florence-2 / BLIP-style icon captioning via local ONNX or Candle
portable_to_ghost.value: The main cost in OmniParser is the Florence-2 caption pass (most of the 0.6-0.8s latency). Ghost could run a smaller caption model (Florence-2 base is ~230MB; BLIP-v2 is larger) via ONNX or HuggingFace Candle (pure Rust ML framework) to generate semantic labels for icons that have no OCR text. This enables Ghost to describe what a button does without a GPU and without an API call, improving ghost_describe_screen quality for icon-heavy UIs.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Candle supports Florence-2 and BLIP. Icon crops are 64x64 - tiny inputs. Batch 128 at once as OmniParser does. This is the highest-effort item but gives Ghost fully offline semantic screen understanding with no API cost per element.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Two-tier grounding: UIA-first with SoM-YOLO fallback, unified confidence score
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost should implement a confidence-gated fallback chain: (1) try UIA - if it returns a matching element with Name/AutomationId, use it directly (zero latency, deterministic); (2) if UIA fails or confidence is low, run YOLO detection + OCR fusion to produce candidate elements, score them against the intent using Windows.Media.Ocr text matching or VLM; (3) if still ambiguous, send full SoM-annotated screenshot to VLM for ID selection. This matches OmniParser's pure-vision path as a last resort while preserving Ghost's UIA speed advantage for standard Windows apps.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already has UIA. Add YOLO as a crate-level service that lazily initializes the ONNX session on first fallback invocation. The confidence scoring can use simple text similarity between intent string and element content strings before escalating to VLM. Keeps VLM API cost near zero for well-behaved apps.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Training data generation pipeline: capture DXGI frames + UIA ground truth to fine-tune a Ghost-specific detector
portable_to_ghost.value: OmniParser's YOLO detector was fine-tuned on 67k web screenshots with DOM-derived boxes. Ghost can generate equivalent training data for Windows desktop apps by: capturing DXGI frames, simultaneously querying UIA for all element bounding boxes on that frame, and saving (image, boxes) pairs. After accumulating ~10k pairs, fine-tune YOLOv8 Nano on Windows-specific UI patterns (Win32 controls, WPF, Electron). This would produce a detector specialized for Ghost's actual target environment rather than web UIs.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost already captures DXGI frames and queries UIA trees. Add a data-collection mode that dumps (PNG, UIA_boxes.json) pairs to disk. Training requires Python/ultralytics but is a one-time offline step. The resulting .pt -> .onnx model is then dropped into Ghost's ort inference path.

pitfalls: SoM requires a VLM call for every grounding decision - there is no local model that replaces GPT-4o for the ID selection step unless you accept significant accuracy loss; Ghost already has this VLM cost, but OmniParser does not eliminate it

YOLOv8 was trained primarily on web screenshots (100k URLs from ClueWeb); detection quality on native Win32, WPF, or game UIs is meaningfully lower and should not be assumed equal to the ScreenSpot numbers
ScreenSpot Pro's 39.6% headline is the VLM+OmniParser combined score, not OmniParser alone; the YOLO detector by itself has no semantic understanding and cannot answer 'which element does X'
The caption model (Florence-2) adds 0.5-0.7s of the total 0.6-0.8s latency; running it on CPU without a GPU will likely push latency to 5-15s per frame, making it unsuitable for fast automation loops
AGPL license on the YOLO component (icon_detect) - if Ghost ships OmniParser weights as part of a commercial product, the AGPL copyleft provisions apply; the Florence-2 caption model is MIT
Overlap removal is O(N^2) in Python with no spatial indexing; for screens with hundreds of elements (complex spreadsheets, IDEs) this becomes a visible bottleneck
EasyOCR (default OCR) is not on-device: it loads a 40MB+ deep learning model and runs on PyTorch; Ghost's Windows.Media.Ocr is dramatically faster and fully on-device - do not adopt EasyOCR
The 64x64 icon crop resize before captioning destroys fine detail in small icons; for high-DPI displays with tiny icons this degrades caption quality
No streaming or incremental output - the full pipeline must complete before any element is returned; there is no early-exit for high-confidence UIA matches
OmniParser has no notion of element state (enabled/disabled, checked/unchecked, focused) - it only detects visual presence; UIA provides this for free on Windows

research.target: SOTA GUI-agent patterns 2025-2026: Anthropic Computer Use, OpenAI CUA/Operator, ScreenSpot-Pro, OSWorld, ACI design
research.what_it_is: This covers a family of GUI automation agent systems built on top of frontier VLMs (Claude, GPT-4o, Qwen2-VL) that perceive desktop or browser screenshots and emit mouse/keyboard actions to complete computer tasks autonomously. The main players are Anthropic Computer Use (Claude-native API tool), OpenAI CUA/Operator (GPT-4o based, 38.1% OSWorld SR), and a rich open-source ecosystem (Agent S2, UFO2, OS-Atlas, UGround/SeeAct-V) benchmarked on OSWorld (369 real desktop tasks, human baseline 72.36%) and ScreenSpot-Pro (1,581 professional-resolution grounding instances). Best task success on OSWorld as of mid-2025 is ~45% (OpenCUA-72B); best grounding on ScreenSpot-Pro is ~60% (OpenCUA-72B), with HyperClick-7B at 48.2%.
research.architecture: Perception -> Grounding -> Planning -> Action -> Verification. (1) PERCEPTION: DXGI/screenshot at every step; optionally UIA/a11y tree queried in parallel as structured overlay. (2) GROUNDING: either (a) monolithic VLM outputs (x,y) pixel coords directly from screenshot + instruction, or (b) separated planner+grounder where frontier LLM generates textual plan and specialist 7B model converts to pixel coords. OmniParser/UFO2 add a pre-grounding stage: YOLO-v8n icon detector + Florence-2 captioner produces DOM-like element list before any LLM call. (3) PLANNING: hierarchical - HostAgent decomposes task into subtasks, AppAgents execute per-subtask with bounded action primitives. Agent S2 proactively replans after every subtask completion. (4) ACTION: bounded discrete primitives (click element_id, type, scroll, hotkey, drag) using stable element IDs where a11y tree available; raw (x,y) in vision-only mode. Anthropic Computer Use tool schema includes: screenshot, left_click, right_click, double_click, left_click_drag, type, key, scroll, zoom (enable_zoom:true for region inspection). (5) VERIFICATION: VeriGUI TVAE cycle - predicted semantic effect at step t becomes verification hypothesis at step t+1; binary SUCCESS/NO_CHANGE detects idempotent failure loops which account for 72.3% of all task failures. Pre-action: VeriSafe autoformalizes user intent to logic spec and verifies action before execution.
research.grounding_approach: Three mechanisms in active use. (1) DIRECT COORDINATE REGRESSION (vision-only): VLM takes screenshot + natural language description, outputs normalized coordinates in 0-1000 range (e.g., '(743, 512)' mapped as x/1000*width, y/1000*height). OS-Atlas (InternVL2-4B or Qwen2-VL-7B fine-tuned on 13M GUI elements across 5 platforms) and UGround (LLaVA-NeXT-7B or Qwen2-VL-72B fine-tuned on 10M elements from 1.3M screenshots) both use this pattern. Outputs are center point or bounding box in 0-1000 space. ReGUIDE improves Qwen-2.5-VL-3B from 55.5% to 87.3% on ScreenSpot. JT-GUIAgent Grounder hits 97.2% on ScreenSpot-V2 mobile text. (2) HYBRID UIA+VISION (UFO2): Query Windows UIA for is_visible()+is_enabled() controls with bounding boxes. Run OmniParser-v2 (YOLO-v8n + Florence-2) on screenshot for custom-rendered elements. Suppress vision detections with >10% IoU against UIA hits; promote remaining to pseudo-UIA objects. LLM picks element_id from unified structured list - no raw coord regression. (3) CASCADED ZOOM-SEARCH (ScreenSeeker): For targets <0.5% of image area on professional high-DPI displays, first use VLM to identify plausible screen region, then crop and send full-resolution sub-image to grounder. 48.1% on ScreenSpot-Pro without additional training vs. 18.9% prior SOTA. Benchmark numbers: GPT-4o on ScreenSpot-Pro: 0.8%; OS-Atlas-7B: 18.9%; OmniParser-v2: 39.5%; HyperClick-7B: 48.2%; OpenCUA-72B: 60.8%.
research.action_space: Anthropic Computer Use (computer_20251124): screenshot (returns base64 PNG), left_click {coordinate:[x,y]}, right_click, double_click, middle_click, left_click_drag {start_coordinate, end_coordinate}, type {text}, key {text xdotool-style}, cursor_position (returns current coords), scroll {coordinate, direction, amount}, zoom {coordinate, size} (requires enable_zoom:true; captures cropped region at full resolution). Max display: 2576px on long edge; 1:1 pixel-to-coordinate mapping. OS-Atlas unified space: 3 basic actions (click, type, scroll) + extensible custom actions (open_app, drag) - minimal to maximize cross-platform knowledge sharing. ACI-based systems (Agent S, UFO2): click(element_id), type(text), hotkey(keys), drag_and_drop(src, dst), scroll(element_id, direction), wait() - element IDs from a11y tree, not raw coords. SeeAct-V: planner outputs textual description, grounder converts to (x,y) via UGround, no element IDs. ActionEngine: synthesizes full executable Python programs from state-machine memory, achieving 95% task success on Reddit/WebArena with single LLM call (vs 66% reactive vision-only, 11.8x cost reduction, 2x latency reduction).
speed_tactics: Two-tier model routing: specialist 3B-7B grounder (UGround/OS-Atlas ONNX) for coordinate prediction, frontier model only for planning. V-Droid achieves 4.3s/step, 0.7s/decision - 6.1x and 32.1x faster than prior mobile agents using this split.

State-machine action caching (ActionEngine): offline exploration builds UI state map; online execution retrieves precompiled Python program - single LLM call for entire task vs N per-step calls; 11.8x cost reduction, 2x latency reduction, 95% SR on WebArena Reddit tasks.

Screen-delta as loop-breaker early-exit: TVAE verification adds ~45% per-step token overhead but breaks idempotent failure loops (72.3% of failures); net trajectory token count can be lower due to eliminated infinite repetition.
UIA tree query before any VLM call: synchronous OS call (<10ms on Windows) filters interactive elements, reduces VLM search space dramatically. UFO2 hybrid recovered 9.86% of previously failed tasks.

Cascaded zoom only when needed: do not send full high-DPI screenshot to VLM for every action. Use full frame for coarse region detection, crop+zoom only when target element is small relative to screen area.

Resolution downscaling discipline: Anthropic Computer Use recommends 1024x768 for API calls (smaller = faster inference); use zoom action for targeted high-res inspection rather than full-resolution every step.

Simple Thinking over Full-Thinking for grounding: UI-AGILE shows elaborate reasoning during grounding degrades accuracy AND doubles inference time. Strict separation - planner reasons, grounder regresses coordinates without reasoning chain.
Planning and reflection account for 75-94% of per-step latency on OSWorld and latency grows with task length. Minimize frontier-model planning calls per step via hierarchical subtask batching.
standout_features: VeriGUI TVAE loop: predicted semantic effect at step t becomes verification hypothesis at step t+1. Breaks infinite idempotent-failure loops (72.3% of all GUI agent failures empirically). VeriGUI-7B achieves 52.5% Recovery Success Rate vs state-of-art baselines; 59.7% on MiniWoB++ for open-source SOTA. False-positive penalty is 4x false-negative during GRPO training, forcing alignment of internal belief with visual reality.

HyperClick verbalized confidence calibration: first grounding model to output confidence alongside coordinates, calibrated via truncated Gaussian reward + Brier score. Achieves 93.7% ScreenSpot-V2, 48.2% ScreenSpot-Pro at 7B scale - outperforming UI-TARS-72B and Aguvis-72B. Enables the agent to refuse to act when confidence is low.

UFO2 GUI+API unified execution: Puppeteer orchestrator chooses between GUI click and native Win32/WinCOM API call per action. API path reduced completion steps by 58.5% and improved SR 6-8% on Office tasks. Validated: 7.1% better than OpenAI Operator on WAA, 2x better on OSWorld-W.

OmniParser pre-parsing layer: YOLO-v8n icon detector + Florence-2 semantic captioner produces DOM-like element list with bounding boxes before any frontier LLM call. Converts free-form screenshot into structured input, significantly simplifying the LLM grounding task. OmniParser-v2 achieved 39.5% on ScreenSpot-Pro SOTA at time of release.

Agent S2 Mixture of Grounding Experts: routes between UI-TARS-72B-DPO (visual elements), Tesseract OCR (text spans), and UNO (structural/a11y). Achieves SOTA on OSWorld with screenshot-only input AND on WindowsAgentArena - demonstrating expert routing beats monolithic approach.

Cascaded search for high-res grounding (ScreenSeeker): identify region -> crop -> ground within crop. +29 percentage points on ScreenSpot-Pro vs best prior model without additional training. Critical for professional/dense UIs.

Anthropic prompt injection classifier: automatically scans every screenshot for adversarial instructions embedded in UI content; triggers human confirmation before proceeding on detected injection. Built-in, API-level, cannot be disabled without explicit opt-out.

portable_to_ghost.idea: TVAE screen-delta verification via pHash after every action
portable_to_ghost.value: Breaks the idempotent failure loop (72.3% of all failures). Ghost captures DXGI frame before and after SendInput, computes perceptual hash (pHash/dHash, 16x16 DCT, <0.1ms). Below-threshold delta = NO_CHANGE failure, triggers recovery path. Simpler than VeriGUI's semantic expected-effect approach because Ghost owns the capture pipeline.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: DXGI capture already exists. Add pHash computation in Rust (image crate + DCT). Expose ghost_wait_for_change(timeout_ms) -> ChangeResult enum. All action MCP tools call this internally before returning. Wire recovery logic: NO_CHANGE triggers re-grounding with alternate description or escalates to ghost_describe_screen for replanning.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Cascaded zoom-grounding for dense/small UI targets
portable_to_ghost.value: ScreenSpot-Pro shows grounding accuracy collapses when target is <0.5% of image area.
