Two-stage: VLM identifies approximate region from full DXGI frame, Ghost crops via ghost_screenshot_region and sends crop to grounder. Eliminates precision loss from downscaling dense toolbars.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: ghost_screenshot_region already exists. Add ghost_locate_region(description) -> BoundingBox that calls VLM for coarse localization. Compose with ghost_locate_by_description on the crop. Coordinate transform: crop_offset_x + local_x = global_x. Route all ghost_find calls through this two-stage path when target_size_hint = small.

portable_to_ghost.idea: UFO2-style UIA+YOLO fusion pipeline
portable_to_ghost.value: Ghost has UIA tree access. UFO2 recovered 9.86% of failed tasks by adding vision-only detections for custom-rendered controls. Add YOLO-v8n ONNX (OmniParser icon_detect model, AGPL, <30ms CPU) to detect elements that bypass UIA. Suppress detections with >10% IoU against UIA hits; promote remainder as pseudo-UIA objects. Feed unified structured element list to VLM instead of raw screenshot.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: UIA crate already traverses tree. Add bounding_box extraction per UIA control. Load YOLO-v8n ONNX via ort crate. IoU filter in Rust is trivial. Output: Vec<UiElement> with {id, type, label, bbox, source: Uia|Vision}. Expose via ghost_describe_screen returning structured JSON. Reduces VLM task from pixel regression to element-list selection.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Local ONNX specialist grounder (OS-Atlas or UGround) as fast path
portable_to_ghost.value: Frontier VLM for coordinate regression costs $0.10-0.40/task and adds 2-10s latency. OS-Atlas-7B and UGround-V1-7B are Qwen2-VL fine-tunes, ONNX-exportable. 73%+ ScreenSpot accuracy at 7B. Ghost calls local ONNX for coordinate prediction, frontier API only for plan generation. 5-10x cost reduction, 3-5x latency reduction per grounding call.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Ghost VLM client already supports multiple providers. Add OnnxGrounder provider backed by ort crate. ghost_locate_by_description and ghost_find route through OnnxGrounder by default. Frontier API invoked only for ghost_execute_intent plan generation. Model download once at install time (~15GB for 7B Q4). Alternatively use NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM for the 90b-vision model already in Ghost's provider list.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Verbalized confidence gate for destructive actions
portable_to_ghost.value: HyperClick shows calibrated confidence at 7B scale is achievable (93.7% ScreenSpot-V2 at 7B). Ghost should request confidence score alongside grounding and refuse to execute ghost_click on destructive/irreversible actions (file delete, form submit, purchase confirm) if confidence < configurable threshold. Prevents misclicks that cause real harm.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Add confidence: f32 to GroundingResult struct. Prompt the VLM to output JSON: {coordinates: [x,y], confidence: 0.0-1.0, reasoning: str}. ghost_click and ghost_click_by_description accept confidence_threshold: f32 parameter. Below threshold: return GroundingLowConfidence error to MCP caller. Add ghost_find_with_confidence tool that exposes confidence for orchestrator-level decisions.

portable_to_ghost.idea: GUI+API hybrid execution (Puppeteer pattern for Office/shell apps)
portable_to_ghost.value: UFO2 reduced steps by 58.5% on Office tasks by calling Win32/COM APIs instead of GUI clicks when semantically equivalent. Ghost should maintain a capability registry per app: before grounding a click, check if a native API achieves the same effect. Even a PowerShell bridge covers most Windows automation needs.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: Define AppCapabilityRegistry: HashMap<AppName, Vec<ApiCapability>> where ApiCapability has {intent_pattern: Regex, executor: Box<dyn Fn(params) -> Result>}. ghost_execute_intent checks registry before grounding. Start with: PowerShell executor for file/OS ops, COM executor for Excel/Word/Outlook (windows crate or com-rs). GUI fallback if API fails. Per-app integration is O(N) work but ROI is massive for power-user workflows.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Proactive subtask-boundary replanning
portable_to_ghost.value: Agent S2's key reliability gain: replan after every subtask completion, not just on failure. Prevents plan drift in multi-app workflows. Ghost's execute_intent should checkpoint screen state and re-evaluate remaining plan at each subtask boundary.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: After each subtask in ghost_execute_intent completes successfully, auto-capture DXGI frame, run ghost_describe_screen_fast, prepend result to next subtask's planning context as 'current state after step N: [description]'. One additional VLM describe call per subtask boundary (~200ms) prevents the 1.4-2.7x step overhead agents currently suffer from stale world models.

portable_to_ghost.idea: Action sequence cache keyed on (app, intent-hash, screen-hash)
portable_to_ghost.value: ActionEngine achieves 95% task success at 11.8x lower cost by caching UI state-machine paths. Ghost can cache successful action sequences and replay without VLM calls when pre/post screen state matches. Gives Ghost a 'muscle memory' layer that accelerates with repeated use.
portable_to_ghost.ghost_fit: SQLite cache: {app_name TEXT, intent_embedding_hash BLOB, pre_screen_phash BLOB, action_sequence BLOB (bincode Vec<GhostAction>), success_count INT}. On task start: embed intent (local embedding model or hash), hash current DXGI frame, query cache. Hit: execute stored sequence with TVAE delta verification at each step. Miss: full VLM planning, store on success. Cache grows from actual usage - zero cold-start cost.

pitfalls: Frontier-model-for-coordinate-regression is uneconomical: $0.10-0.40/task, 2-10x slower than specialist 7B grounder. Grounding accuracy does NOT scale with model size (GPT-4o: 0.8% on ScreenSpot-Pro; 7B specialist: 18.9%). Always separate planner (frontier) from grounder (specialist).
UIA-alone misses 9.86% of tasks: custom-rendered, canvas, and game-engine UI bypasses accessibility tree entirely. Never rely solely on UIA tree without a vision fallback layer.

Screenshot downscaling destroys accuracy for professional UIs: target elements are 0.07% of image area on high-DPI displays. Blindly downscaling to 1024px causes near-zero grounding accuracy on dense toolbars. Use cascaded zoom-crop instead.

No-Thinking grounding models fail on non-grounding actions; Full-Thinking models degrade grounding accuracy and double training/inference time (UI-AGILE empirical finding). Use Simple Thinking or strict planner-grounder separation.

No screen-delta check = infinite failure loops: 72.3% of all execution failures across 1,265 tasks were idempotent loops. An action loop without NO_CHANGE detection will appear to run but accomplish nothing.

Set-of-Marks without a detector is ineffective: SoM prompting does not work for web agents without HTML/a11y backing to generate the bounding boxes. Do not overlay numbered boxes on raw screenshots without OmniParser-level element detection first.

Prompt injection via on-screen content: frontier models comply with dangerous GUI actions 90% of the time without jailbreaking. Web pages and documents can embed adversarial instructions. Implement injection detection for all screenshot processing paths, especially before destructive actions.

Top agents take 1.4-2.7x more steps than humans; planning+reflection is 75-94% of per-step latency and grows with task length. Naively chaining per-step frontier calls makes long-horizon tasks unusable. Batch subtasks, cache known paths.

Knowledge-execution gap: even 90% external knowledge accuracy yields only 41% execution success rate (OSWorld/UI-Evol). Verification and recovery loops are the primary reliability mechanism, not knowledge quality alone.

audit.purpose: Low-level Win32/UIA/SendInput/DXGI/OCR primitive layer for the Ghost desktop automation framework. Provides: keyboard and mouse input via SendInput, screen capture via DXGI desktop duplication, UIA tree walking (live walker + cached walker), an STA thread pool for COM/UIA work, a WinEvent-based event bus, on-device OCR via Windows.Media.Ocr, and idle detection via frame hashing.
strengths: DXGI device and context are cached in a Mutex<Option<CachedCapture>> (screen.rs:19) so D3D11CreateDevice and adapter enumeration only pay once - subsequent screenshots avoid COM init overhead.

CachedTreeWalker (cached_walker.rs) batches all property fetches into a single FindAllBuildCache round-trip with a pre-built IUIAutomationCacheRequest, eliminating the per-element IPC penalty that kills naive walkers.

StaPool (sta_pool.rs) correctly isolates UIA on COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED worker threads with a circuit breaker (3 panics in 60s) and per-job timeout (default 30s), preventing a hung COM call from blocking the async runtime.
EventBus (event_bus.rs) is event-driven via SetWinEventHook + Notify, not a polling loop; wait_for_change correctly handles spurious wakeups by re-checking seq after each notify.
capture_screen_region (screen.rs:63) supports windowed crop + downscale + JPEG encoding in one call, enabling 10-50x smaller vision payloads vs full PNG.
OCR is fully on-device via Windows.Media.Ocr (ocr.rs) - no API key, no network call, ~50-200ms; find_text_local is a clean alternative to vision fallback for plain-text matching.
BackgroundClicker (postmessage.rs) uses PostMessage so it never steals foreground focus - correctly identified as the safe click path for background windows.
type_text (keyboard.rs:50) sends KEYEVENTF_UNICODE so any Unicode character including emoji can be typed without layout dependency.
Emergency stop via Ctrl+Alt+G (hotkey.rs) is registered on a dedicated thread and calls release_all_modifiers to prevent stuck keys after abort.

weaknesses.issue: Vision provider env vars (NVIDIA_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) are missing from the ghost MCP server registration
weaknesses.why: C:/Users/Krist/.claude/mcp.json registers the ghost server with no 'env' block. ghost-mcp.exe inherits the Claude Code process environment which does not carry these variables. pick_provider() in vision.rs:96 immediately returns Err(GhostError::Config(...)) on every ghost_locate_by_description / ghost_click_by_description call, making vision-based grounding completely non-functional even though the code is correct.

weaknesses.issue: SendInput keyboard input races with Claude Code terminal stealing focus between tool calls
weaknesses.why: type_text (keyboard.rs:50) and press_key (keyboard.rs:67) call SendInput which delivers input to whichever window has keyboard focus at injection time. In a Claude Code tool loop, the MCP server process runs inside a terminal (or a VSCode/Windows Terminal pane) that can regain focus between tool responses. There is no focus assertion before SendInput - no SetForegroundWindow, no AttachThreadInput, no check that the intended HWND is still foreground. Ghost has focus_window (tree.rs:279) and BackgroundClicker but nothing wires them into the keyboard path. A ghost_type call preceded by ghost_click_at has a ~5-200ms window where terminal focus steal causes keystrokes to land in the wrong window.

weaknesses.issue: Live tree walker (tree.rs) calls ControlViewWalker() inside every recursive step, creating a fresh COM proxy per node-visit
weaknesses.why: get_walker() at tree.rs:97 calls self.automation.ControlViewWalker() on every invocation. It is called inside search_subtree_by_name (line 111), search_subtree_by_role (line 131), and collect_interactive (line 213) - once per tree node. ControlViewWalker is a cross-process COM call returning a new proxy object. On a typical desktop with 200-1000 UIA nodes this inflates the IPC count massively. The IUIAutomationTreeWalker should be created once per search, not per node.

weaknesses.issue: CachedTreeWalker.find_by_name and find_by_role walk the entire desktop even when a foreground scope is sufficient
weaknesses.why: cached_walker.rs:86 and 99 both call self.walk(&root) where root is always GetRootElement() - the full desktop. Unlike UiaTree which has find_by_name_fast (tree.rs:72) that scopes to the foreground window first, CachedTreeWalker has no fast path. A single FindAllBuildCache(TreeScope_Subtree, TrueCondition) on the desktop root in a complex UI session can block for 500ms-2s.

weaknesses.issue: DXGI capture creates a new staging texture and calls DuplicateOutput on every single screenshot
weaknesses.why: capture_rgba (screen.rs:158) calls DuplicateOutput (line 164) every invocation, then CreateTexture2D for a staging surface (line 189). IDXGIOutputDuplication is a session-scoped object with internal frame queuing - recreating it every call throws away the duplication state, requiring a fresh keyframe from the driver on next AcquireNextFrame. The staging texture is also allocated and released every call (no pool). For agent loops calling ghost_wait_for_idle with stable_frames=3, this means 3+ full DXGI sessions per wait. Correct pattern: hold the IDXGIOutputDuplication across calls; only re-create if DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST.

weaknesses.issue: IdleDetector.wait_stable hashes full PNG-encoded bytes, not raw pixels, adding encoder overhead to every poll iteration
weaknesses.why: idle.rs:72 calls capture_screen() which returns a PNG-encoded Vec<u8>, then hashes the PNG bytes. PNG encoding a 1920x1080 frame takes 50-300ms. wait_stable polls every 16ms (line 82) but spends most of that time PNG-encoding. The correct implementation is to hash the raw RGBA frame (or the 4x4 downsample output from downsample_to_4x4 which exists in the same file at line 15) using the already-available capture_screen_full_rgba() path. The IdleDetector struct comment says 'Stub DXGI-backed idle detector' - it is not actually using the DXGI idle strategy described in the module doc.

weaknesses.issue: ocr.rs blocks the async runtime by calling IAsyncOperation::get() (a blocking WinRT spin-wait) inside a tokio task
weaknesses.why: block_on_async at ocr.rs:159 calls op.get() which is the synchronous WinRT blocking get - it spins the current thread until the operation completes. This is called from capture_and_ocr (ocr.rs:32) which is in turn called from the MCP dispatch path on the tokio thread pool. Blocking a tokio worker thread for 50-200ms (OCR latency) stalls other async tasks sharing that thread. The OCR call should be offloaded to tokio::task::spawn_blocking or the STA pool.

weaknesses.issue: EventBus only subscribes to EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND; focus changes and UIA structure changes are not observed
weaknesses.why: pump_thread (event_bus.rs:97) hooks only EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND..EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND. A click inside an already-foreground app, a text field appearing after a button press, or a dialog opening without changing the foreground window will not bump the seq counter. wait_for_event will time out or return stale data in these very common agent-loop patterns. The module doc at line 10 explicitly flags this: 'Future work: subscribe to UIA structure-changed and focus-changed events'. UIA AddFocusChangedEventHandler and AddStructureChangedEventHandler would cover the gap.

weaknesses.issue: UiaElement.role_id_to_name uses wrong numeric constants for most control types
weaknesses.why: element.rs:73: 'button' is mapped to id 50, 'edit' to 42. The actual UIA_ButtonControlTypeId is 50000, UIA_EditControlTypeId is 50004. The values 50 and 42 do not correspond to any real UIA control type. As a result, role-based find, describe_screen filtering on INTERACTIVE_ROLES, and collect_interactive (tree.rs:198) will never match any element by role in production - they will all fall through to 'unknown'. Only element descriptors from CachedTreeWalker.descriptor_from_cached (which reads CachedControlType and passes through to role_id_to_name) will exhibit this bug; all role-based ghost_find calls are broken.

weaknesses.issue: COM apartment mismatch: UiaTree is created in a COINIT_MULTITHREADED (MTA) apartment but IUIAutomation is a STA COM server
weaknesses.why: uia/mod.rs:19 calls CoInitializeEx(None, COINIT_MULTITHREADED) for the main/tokio threads. UiaTree::new() (tree.rs:19) calls CoCreateInstance on those MTA threads. IUIAutomation (CUIAutomation8) is an in-proc STA server. Creating an STA server from an MTA thread forces COM to marshal all method calls through a hidden proxy/stub, adding a round-trip per UIA call and producing RPC_E_WRONG_THREAD or silent failures under contention. The STA pool correctly uses COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED but UiaTree - used directly by GhostSession without the pool - does not.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot always returns full-screen PNG base64 with no size cap - will overflow context windows
weaknesses.why: The ghost_screenshot tool (mcp/main.rs:169) always calls session.screenshot(Region::full()) which calls capture_screen() which encodes a full-resolution PNG (typically 3-8 MB for a 1080p desktop) then base64-encodes it (4/3 overhead = 4-11 MB string). Claude's context window has a hard limit; a 1080p PNG base64 string alone exceeds Claude Haiku's context. The tool schema description says 'full screen, lossless' with no mention of alternatives. Callers in agent loops will habitually reach for ghost_screenshot; ghost_screenshot_region exists but is not the default.

weaknesses.issue: postmessage.rs lparam encoding uses signed bit-masking that is wrong for coordinates > 32767
weaknesses.why: BackgroundClicker::click at postmessage.rs:20: `((y & 0xFFFF) << 16 | (x & 0xFFFF))` operates on i32. For a 4K monitor with y > 32767 the upper bit of the 16-bit field is set, making y appear negative when the receiving window decodes HIWORD(lparam) as a signed short. The standard Win32 idiom is MAKELPARAM which uses u16 packing. This causes clicks to land at wrong y positions on high-DPI or 4K displays.

weaknesses.issue: patterns.rs set_value fallback clicks to focus then types without verifying focus was acquired
weaknesses.why: set_value (patterns.rs:39) when ValuePattern is unavailable: clicks the bounding rect center, then immediately calls type_text. There is no sleep, no UIA focus check, and no verification that the click succeeded. On slow applications or during animations, type_text fires while the field is still gaining focus, dropping the first character(s). Combined with the SendInput focus-race issue, this is a compound failure mode.

weaknesses.issue: mouse.rs to_absolute() calls GetSystemMetrics on every mouse event and has no DPI-per-monitor awareness
weaknesses.why: to_absolute (mouse.rs:7) calls GetSystemMetrics(SM_CXSCREEN/SM_CYSCREEN) on each invocation. This is a live syscall per click/hover/scroll event. More critically, SM_CXSCREEN returns the primary monitor's virtual screen size in logical pixels, which on a multi-monitor or per-monitor DPI setup does not match the MOUSEEVENTF_ABSOLUTE coordinate space (which spans the full virtual desktop). Clicks on secondary monitors will land at wrong positions.

weaknesses.issue: bgra_to_rgba pixel conversion loop is scalar; a 1920x1080 frame does ~8M byte operations without SIMD
weaknesses.why: bgra_to_rgba (screen.rs:217) iterates pixel-by-pixel with a nested loop. At 1080p (2,073,600 pixels) this is ~8M byte reads/writes in a tight loop. The BGRA->RGBA swap is a 4-lane byte shuffle, trivially vectorizable. The `image` crate dependency is already present and could handle this, or a simple bytemuck/unsafe cast + u32 rotate would be 4-8x faster.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add NVIDIA_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GHOST_VISION_PROVIDER, and GHOST_VISION_MODEL to the ghost server's 'env' block in ~/.claude/mcp.json so vision tools actually work
improvement_opportunities.impact: Restores ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, and ghost_type_by_description from completely broken to functional. Zero code changes required - pure config fix. This is the single highest-impact one-line change in the project.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Fix role_id_to_name constants in element.rs to use actual UIA control type IDs (UIA_ButtonControlTypeId=50000, UIA_EditControlTypeId=50004, etc.) from the windows crate constants
improvement_opportunities.impact: Restores role-based element finding (ghost_find with role=, ghost_describe_screen role filtering) which is currently matching nothing. This fixes a silent correctness bug where every role query returns 'unknown' and falls through.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Assert/acquire target HWND focus before SendInput keyboard delivery: after ghost_click_at or ghost_click, call GetForegroundWindow() in a short retry loop (max ~100ms) to confirm the intended window is foreground before firing type_text or press_key
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the terminal-focus-steal race that causes keystrokes from Claude Code tool calls to land in the wrong window. This is the root cause of 'misfires from Claude Code' in agent loops. A focused SetForegroundWindow + brief AttachThreadInput confirmation costs <5ms and makes type reliable.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Hold IDXGIOutputDuplication across screenshot calls (cache it in CachedCapture alongside the device/context); recreate only on DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST or DXGI_ERROR_SESSION_DISCONNECTED
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates DuplicateOutput + CreateTexture2D overhead on every screenshot. AcquireNextFrame can return a frame in <1ms when the duplication session is already open; vs 15-50ms for session setup. For wait_for_idle polling at 16ms intervals this is a >2x speedup of the idle-detection loop.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Fix IdleDetector.wait_stable to hash raw RGBA pixels (via capture_screen_full_rgba + downsample_to_4x4) instead of PNG-encoded bytes
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates 50-300ms PNG encoding from every idle-detection poll frame. downsample_to_4x4 already exists in idle.rs and produces a 64-byte fingerprint; hashing that is microseconds. This makes wait_for_idle actually responsive at the 16ms poll interval instead of 100-400ms.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Cache the IUIAutomationTreeWalker at UiaTree construction time rather than calling ControlViewWalker() inside every recursive search step
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces cross-process COM round-trips in live tree walks by 1 per node (on a 500-node subtree that is 500 extra COM calls saved per search). Cuts describe_screen latency by 30-60% on complex applications.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Offload OCR (capture_and_ocr / run_ocr / block_on_async) to tokio::task::spawn_blocking or the existing STA pool to avoid blocking tokio worker threads
improvement_opportunities.impact: Prevents 50-200ms stalls on tokio worker threads during OCR calls. In agent loops that interleave ghost_find_text_local with other async ops, this unblocks concurrent MCP requests that would otherwise queue behind the blocking OCR call.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Extend EventBus to subscribe to IUIAutomation focus-changed and structure-changed events (AddFocusChangedEventHandler, AddStructureChangedEventHandler) on the STA pool thread
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes ghost_wait_for_event actually reactive to in-app state changes (dialog opens, text field appears, button enables) rather than only foreground-window switches. Today an agent using wait_for_event after a ghost_click always times out for in-app transitions. This is the mechanism that makes event-driven waits useful instead of deceptive.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a foreground-scoped fast path to CachedTreeWalker.find_by_name and find_by_role: try ElementFromHandle(GetForegroundWindow()) + FindAllBuildCache on that subtree first, fall back to desktop root only on miss
improvement_opportunities.impact: Mirrors the fast-path already present in the live UiaTree. On a typical agent loop where the target is always in the focused window, this reduces cached tree walk time from 500ms-2s (full desktop) to 20-100ms (foreground subtree).

improvement_opportunities.idea: Fix postmessage.rs LPARAM encoding to use u16-safe packing: `LPARAM((((y as u16) as isize) << 16) | (x as u16) as isize)` to handle coordinates > 32767 on 4K / multi-monitor setups
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes BackgroundClicker on high-resolution displays where y > 32767. Currently clicks land at wrong positions on 4K monitors or when the target window is below the midpoint of a 4K screen.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Replace the ghost_screenshot tool's default behavior to use ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true + max_dim=768 + jpeg_quality=75, or at minimum add a size_bytes field and a strong warning in the tool description about context-window overflow
improvement_opportunities.impact: Prevents context-window exhaustion in agent loops. A 1080p full-screen PNG base64 string is 4-11 MB; Claude's effective context for reasoning collapses when a single tool result is that large. The infrastructure to produce compact region screenshots already exists - ghost_screenshot just does not use it.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Move UiaTree construction (and all direct IUIAutomation calls in GhostSession) to run inside the STA pool threads rather than the MTA-initialized main/tokio threads
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the COM apartment mismatch between CUIAutomation8 (STA server) and COINIT_MULTITHREADED caller threads. Removes hidden COM marshaling proxy overhead on every UIA call from GhostSession. Makes UIA calls both faster and more correct under concurrent access.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Replace scalar bgra_to_rgba with a u32-rotate SIMD-friendly loop: read each pixel as u32, rotate bytes, write back; or delegate to image::DynamicImage conversion which can use SIMD paths
improvement_opportunities.impact: 4-8x faster pixel format conversion at 1080p (saves ~10-30ms per full-screen capture). Small gain per call but compounds in idle-detection polling loops.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a focus-acquisition verification step in patterns.rs set_value fallback: after the click-to-focus, poll UIA CurrentHasFocus or GetForegroundWindow for up to 150ms before calling type_text
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates dropped first characters when typing into fields that need time to gain focus (file dialogs, browser address bars, Electron inputs). This is a frequent cause of partial text entry failures in agent loops.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Fix mouse.rs to_absolute() to use GetSystemMetrics(SM_CXVIRTUALSCREEN) / SM_CYVIRTUALSCREEN for the MOUSEEVENTF_ABSOLUTE coordinate space, and cache the result with a dirty flag reset on WM_DISPLAYCHANGE
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes absolute mouse coordinate mapping on multi-monitor systems where SM_CXSCREEN (primary only) underestimates the virtual desktop, causing clicks on secondary monitors to land at wrong positions. Also eliminates one syscall per mouse event.

audit.purpose: Safe async session API that wraps ghost-core primitives (UIA tree walking, SendInput, DXGI screen capture, Windows OCR) and exposes them as a high-level automation session used by the ghost-mcp stdio server (Claude Code tool loop driver). It owns the GhostSession struct, the By locator enum, GhostElement, vision.rs (multi-provider VLM fallback via NVIDIA/Anthropic), and macro-style compound primitives: navigate_and_wait, fill_form, click_and_wait_for_text.
strengths: EventBus (event_bus.rs:24-79) uses a Tokio Notify + atomic seq to provide edge-triggered wakeups on foreground change, eliminating pure-polling in find(), click_text_local(), click_and_wait_for_text(), and wait_for_event(). Backoff ladder (25/75/150ms) keeps latency low.

vision.rs implements a full dual-provider VLM path (NVIDIA OpenAI-compat + Anthropic) with automatic key-based provider selection, robust JSON extraction (prose, code-fence, bare-object), and coordinate scaling via Crop::to_screen(). JPEG-at-80 + 1024px cap keeps payloads small.
IdleDetector (idle.rs) uses DXGI duplication + 4x4 downsampled blake3 hash to detect visual stability without any polling cost: only 64 bytes per hash comparison.

find() (session.rs:141-197) tries the foreground window subtree first (find_by_name_fast / find_by_role_fast), falling back to full desktop walk only on miss, giving a practical 10-100x speedup for the common case.
ghost_batch_actions in the MCP layer (main.rs:326-348) lets the agent collapse a whole action sequence into one stdio round-trip, eliminating per-tool JSON-RPC overhead in a Claude Code loop.
Emergency stop (Ctrl+Alt+G) is threaded through every input primitive via is_stopped() and releases modifier keys, giving a safe abort path for runaway automation.
StaPool (sta_pool.rs) provides a circuit-breaker for COM worker panics: 3 panics in 60s opens the circuit, preventing hang avalanche from a crashed UIA server.

weaknesses.issue: No focus verification before SendInput: keyboard input fires into whatever window Win32 says is foreground at the instant SendInput is called
weaknesses.why: In a Claude Code tool loop each MCP call crosses a stdio round-trip. Between tool returns the Claude Code terminal can silently reclaim focus (typing into the terminal, VSCode focus, notifications). type_text() (keyboard.rs:50-64) calls SendInput per character with no foreground-HWND check. fill_form() (session.rs:573-585) calls el.click() then immediately type_text() with no delay and no post-click focus confirmation. A 50-200ms inter-character gap plus any async yield is enough for the terminal to steal the focus back. The only guard is is_stopped(), which is an emergency flag, not a focus check.

weaknesses.issue: focus_window() calls SetForegroundWindow with no confirmation wait and no retry
weaknesses.why: tree.rs:279-288 calls SetForegroundWindow and returns immediately. SetForegroundWindow is advisory on Windows 10/11: it is silently ignored if the calling process has not been the foreground process recently (UIPI foreground lock). No sleep, no AttachThreadInput, no verification that GetForegroundWindow() == target HWND after the call. navigate_and_wait (session.rs:517) chains focus_window -> Ctrl+L -> type_text -> Enter: if focus never transferred, Ctrl+L goes to the terminal and the URL is typed there.

weaknesses.issue: NVIDIA_API_KEY not propagated into ghost-mcp env block: vision is silently dead in the Claude Code direct registration path
weaknesses.why: vision.rs:96-115 (pick_provider) reads NVIDIA_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the process environment at call time. The user note states the key is set only in the comet-mcp wrapper env, not in the direct ghost MCP registration env block. The ghost-mcp process therefore sees neither key, pick_provider() returns Err(Config(...)), and every ghost_locate_by_description / ghost_click_by_description / ghost_type_by_description call returns a Config error. There is no warning at startup: GhostSession::new() does not probe for vision keys, so the failure is invisible until the first vision tool call.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot returns full-screen PNG (main.rs:170-171) with no downscale: a typical 1920x1080 PNG is 2-5 MB which base64-encodes to 3-7 MB of JSON
weaknesses.why: capture_screen() (screen.rs:30-37) encodes the entire desktop as PNG. The MCP response embeds this as a base64 string. At 3-7 MB per call this saturates the MCP stdout buffer, balloons context window consumption, and causes multi-second serialization delays. ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true + jpeg_quality=75 + max_dim=768 is the correct tool, but it is a separate tool: the agent must know to use it. The description of ghost_screenshot says 'full screen, lossless' without discouraging use or pointing to the alternative.

weaknesses.issue: IdleDetector::wait_stable (idle.rs:61-85) hashes raw PNG bytes rather than the decoded pixel buffer, so re-encoding variance between otherwise identical frames can prevent stable detection
weaknesses.why: wait_stable() calls capture_screen() (which returns PNG bytes), then calls hash_frame() on the raw PNG-encoded bytes. PNG encoding is deterministic for identical pixel data with the same encoder state, but the crate comment and code structure suggest the intent was to compare pixel content. The bigger issue is that capture_screen() re-acquires DuplicateOutput on every call (screen.rs:164), meaning each frame is a new desktop duplication session. Under display compositor activity (cursor blink, clock tick, notification badge) the captured frame pixel data changes every 16ms, so stable_frames=3 may require more wall time than the timeout allows, causing spurious wait_for_idle timeouts in navigate_and_wait.

weaknesses.issue: collect_interactive (tree.rs:187-220) re-acquires the UIA TreeWalker on every node traversal, creating N+1 COM calls per describe_screen call
weaknesses.why: get_walker() (tree.rs:97-100) calls self.automation.ControlViewWalker() each time collect_interactive recurses. A typical application window has 100-500 nodes, meaning 100-500 extra COM cross-apartment calls per describe_screen. Each ControlViewWalker() round-trips to the COM server. This is a 2-5x slowdown vs acquiring the walker once at the root. describe_screen_fast() has the same issue.

weaknesses.issue: By::Description is accepted at the API level but immediately rejected by find() with an error directing callers to use a different method
weaknesses.why: locator.rs defines By::Description and session.rs:141-146 immediately matches it out and returns Err(Vision(...)). The By::role() / By::name() fallback-then-vision pattern that the doc comment implies does not exist: there is no automatic escalation to vision if UIA misses. Callers using By::Description with find() get an opaque error rather than the intended vision fallback. fill_form (session.rs:573-585) and click_and_wait_for_text (session.rs:527-565) only call self.find(by), so they also silently fail if passed By::Description.

weaknesses.issue: wait_until condition state exposes only cache_seq and last_error=null; last_error is always Null regardless of previous action outcome
weaknesses.why: session.rs:95-98 builds the condition state as {cache_seq: u64, last_error: Null} unconditionally. The field is never populated with actual error context because GhostSession holds no per-action error state. An agent trying to abort on error via wait_until with a last_error condition will always see null and will never trigger, making the feature non-functional as documented.

weaknesses.issue: EventBus only hooks EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND; UIA structure-changed and focus-changed events are not subscribed
weaknesses.why: event_bus.rs:99-107 hooks a single win event. UIA changes (new elements, value changes, selection changes) do not bump the bus seq. find() and click_and_wait_for_text() poll with backoff waiting for seq to advance; if the target element appears due to UIA structure change (e.g. a dialog box opens programmatically without a foreground change) the bus does not fire and callers spin at their current backoff interval until the timer wakes them. The comment at line 9 acknowledges this as future work but the gap causes missed wakeups in practice.

weaknesses.issue: capture_rgba acquires a new DuplicateOutput per call (screen.rs:164) and allocates a full staging texture per frame
weaknesses.why: DuplicateOutput is an expensive COM call that creates a new desktop duplication session. It is called on every screenshot (and on every wait_stable iteration). A 1920x1080 capture also allocates a full staging ID3D11Texture2D each frame and discards it after memcpy, causing GC pressure. The D3D device/context/output1 are cached in CAPTURE_STATE but DuplicateOutput is explicitly not cached (line 14-15 comment says so but the consequence is per-call overhead of ~5-20ms).

weaknesses.issue: set_value fallback in patterns.rs (line 40-44) clicks element center then calls type_text without clearing existing content first
weaknesses.why: When ValuePattern is unavailable, set_value() clicks the element to focus it and then types text. If the field already has content this appends or inserts at cursor rather than replacing. The agent has no indication whether ValuePattern was used (clean replace) or the fallback was taken (potentially corrupting the field value). fill_form() has no Ctrl+A before type_text in this path.

weaknesses.issue: GhostSession is not Send; tokio::main runs on a multi-threaded runtime but all session methods are called from one logical async task with COM objects that have STA affinity
weaknesses.why: GhostSession holds a UiaTree which holds IUIAutomation (a COM pointer). COM STA objects must be used on the thread that created them. The session is created on the main thread and used from async handlers within the single-threaded logical path in main.rs, which works as long as no spawn() crosses the COM pointer. The element.rs comment (line 26-27) documents this. However, if anyone wraps GhostSession in Arc and spawns, UB results. Adding a negative Send bound or a thread-id guard would make this a compile-time guarantee instead of a convention.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add focus_window_verified(): after SetForegroundWindow, poll GetForegroundWindow() == target HWND with 5ms sleep up to 200ms, with AttachThreadInput fallback. Add a hwnd_guard: Option<HWND> to every keyboard action that re-checks the foreground window before each SendInput call and re-focuses if it drifted. Wire this into fill_form, navigate_and_wait, and hotkey.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Directly fixes the #1 failure mode in Claude Code loops: keystrokes going to the terminal. Eliminates the entire class of 'typed into wrong window' bugs without adding wall-clock delay in the happy path.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Propagate NVIDIA_API_KEY (and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) into ghost-mcp's Claude Code MCP registration env block, and add a startup vision probe in GhostSession::new() that logs a tracing::warn when neither key is present. The probe costs zero latency (pure env::var check).
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes vision being completely dead in direct MCP registration without the comet wrapper. Makes the failure surface at server startup as a visible warning rather than silently failing on the first vision tool call 30 seconds into a task.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Subscribe to IUIAutomation::AddStructureChangedEventHandler and AddFocusChangedEventHandler in event_bus.rs on the pump thread, routing them through EventBus::bump(). This is one-time registration on the automation object.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes find() and click_and_wait_for_text() wake up within 5ms of a UIA structure change (dialog opens, list populates) instead of waiting for the next backoff interval (up to 150ms). Eliminates the most common cause of spurious find-timeouts in fast UI transitions.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Replace By::Description rejection in find() with automatic UIA-first-then-vision escalation: try find_by_name_fast(), and if it returns None within a configurable fraction of the timeout, fall through to locate_by_description(). Remove the error path.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes By::Description actually work as a locator. Agents can write a single find(By::description(...)) call that gracefully handles both UIA-accessible and canvas-rendered elements without knowing which backend to pick.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Cache DuplicateOutput across calls by moving it into CachedCapture and re-acquiring only on DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST. Also reuse the staging texture by pre-allocating at the observed display resolution and resizing only when resolution changes.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces per-screenshot overhead from ~10-20ms (DuplicateOutput + CreateTexture2D) to ~2-4ms (AcquireNextFrame only). Directly speeds up wait_stable (every 16ms poll) and any agent loop that reads the screen to verify action outcomes.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Acquire the UIA TreeWalker once per describe_screen/find call (at the call site before recursion) and pass it as a parameter through search_subtree_by_name and collect_interactive instead of calling get_walker() on every recursive node.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates N ControlViewWalker() COM calls per tree walk (N = number of nodes visited). For a 300-node application window this is 300 fewer COM cross-apartment calls, reducing describe_screen latency by an estimated 30-60ms.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Deprecate ghost_screenshot in the tool description as 'too large for context; use ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true, max_dim=768, jpeg_quality=75'. Change the default in ghost_screenshot itself to auto-crop to the foreground window and JPEG-encode at quality 80 with max_dim=1024, returning size_bytes alongside the image.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Cuts typical screenshot payload from 3-7 MB (PNG full desktop) to 30-80 KB (JPEG foreground crop), reducing MCP round-trip serialization time and preventing context window saturation in long agent loops.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Populate last_error in wait_until state: add a Mutex<Option<String>> field to GhostSession, set it on every method that returns Err, and expose it in the JSONLogic state object. Alternatively add a structured result type to ghost_batch_actions that the agent can poll.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes condition-based abort in wait_until actually functional. Agents can write 'wait_until last_error is null' or 'abort if last_error contains timeout' without manual error tracking.

improvement_opportunities.idea: In set_value fallback path (patterns.rs line 40-44): after clicking to focus, send Ctrl+A then Delete before type_text to clear existing content. Add a `clear_first: bool` parameter to set_value and surface it through GhostElement::type_text and the fill_form API.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes silent data corruption in fill_form when ValuePattern is unavailable (common in Electron, custom Win32, Qt apps). Makes the fallback path idempotent: re-running a failed fill_form does not double-append.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Switch wait_stable (idle.rs) to hash the decoded pixel buffer (use capture_screen_full_rgba and downsample_to_4x4) instead of the raw PNG bytes. Reuse a single DuplicateOutput session across the polling loop rather than re-acquiring on every 16ms tick.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes visual idle detection correct and ~5x faster per poll iteration, reducing spurious wait_for_idle timeouts in navigate_and_wait and post-click stabilization waits.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a ghost_focus_window_verified MCP tool (or make focus_window block until GetForegroundWindow matches) and document it as the required predecessor for any keyboard action. Expose expected_hwnd as a return value from ghost_focus_window so the agent can pass it to a subsequent ghost_wait_for_event or ghost_wait_until.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Gives the Claude Code agent a race-free handle on window focus state. The agent loop can confirm focus before issuing Ctrl+L or type operations, eliminating the most common source of silent automation failures in a multi-window desktop environment.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a ghost_action_result MCP tool that returns {last_action, ok, error, foreground_hwnd, foreground_title} without performing any action. Make GhostSession track the last action name and outcome in an AtomicCell. Use this as self-verification: after every ghost_click or ghost_type the agent can call ghost_action_result to confirm the action was applied to the intended window.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Provides a lightweight verification primitive that closes the feedback loop for focus races without requiring a full screenshot. A single JSON object (~100 bytes) costs nothing in context budget.

audit.purpose: MCP server binary exposing 47 Ghost desktop-automation tools over stdio JSON-RPC. Bridges Claude Code tool calls to Windows UIA tree walking, SendInput keyboard/mouse, DXGI screen capture, WinRT OCR, and vision-model fallback (NVIDIA/Anthropic). Single source file (src/main.rs, ~995 LOC) with all tool dispatch, schema declaration, and base64 encoding. Depends on ghost-session (safe async API) which delegates to ghost-core (raw Win32/UIA), ghost-cache (UIA snapshot mirror), and ghost-intent (FSM intent compiler).
strengths: Transport is never crash-able: every parse error and every handle() Err returns a well-formed JSON-RPC error response; stdout is BufWriter-flushed per message; stdin EOF breaks the loop cleanly.
sonic-rs fallback encoding (main.rs:113-119) gives 3-5x serialization speedup on large describe_screen payloads with a safe serde_json fallback - the right tradeoff.

event_bus.rs EventBus uses a tokio Notify + atomic seq counter, giving sub-5ms event-driven wakeups instead of spin-polling in find(), click_and_wait_for_text(), click_text_local(), and wait_for_event() - a structural win over naive sleep loops.

ghost_screenshot_region exposes max_dim + jpeg_quality so the agent can opt into 10-50x smaller payloads; the tool description even includes the canonical vision invocation (foreground=true, max_dim=768, jpeg_quality=75).
ghost_batch_actions lets an agent collapse a known N-step sequence into one MCP round-trip, eliminating N-1 stdio serialization cycles - critical for Claude Code latency budgets.
STA pool (sta_pool.rs) has panic recovery, circuit breaker (3 panics/60s), and per-job timeout, so a single misbehaving COM element cannot dead-lock the async runtime.
ghost_screenshot full-screen path now deprecated in favor of ghost_screenshot_region; the schema description actively steers agents away from full PNG.
ghost_describe_screen_fast scopes the UIA walk to the foreground HWND only, making it 5-50x faster for the common case; the schema description correctly designates it the recommended default.

By::Description vision fallback is a first-class locator with provider auto-detection (NVIDIA preferred, Anthropic fallback, or GHOST_VISION_PROVIDER override) and robust JSON extraction that handles code fences and inline prose.
All keyboard paths (type_text, press_key, hotkey, key_down, key_up) check is_stopped() before each SendInput call, providing a clean Ctrl+Alt+G abort path mid-sequence.

weaknesses.issue: NVIDIA_API_KEY (and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) not in MCP registration env block
weaknesses.why: settings.json mcpServers.ghost has no 'env' key at all (line 302-305). The pick_provider() function in vision.rs reads std::env::var("NVIDIA_API_KEY") and std::env::var("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY") at runtime. When ghost-mcp.exe is spawned by Claude Code as an MCP child process, it inherits only the Claude Code terminal environment - which does not include NVIDIA_API_KEY unless the system-level env or the MCP 'env' block provides it. The comet-mcp wrapper (mentioned in the audit brief) passes NVIDIA_API_KEY via its own env block, but the direct ghost registration does not. Result: every call to ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, or ghost_type_by_description will fail with GhostError::Config('no vision provider configured') at pick_provider() (vision.rs:112-114), making the entire vision subsystem silently dead when driven through Claude Code's tool loop.

weaknesses.issue: JSON-RPC error objects are missing the required 'code' integer field
weaknesses.why: The JSON-RPC 2.0 spec requires error objects to be {code: integer, message: string, data?: any}. Ghost emits {message: string} only (main.rs:98-102 and parse-error path at line 73-83). MCP clients that strictly validate error envelopes will fail to parse errors; more importantly, the agent has no way to distinguish parse errors (-32700), method-not-found (-32601), invalid params (-32602), or application errors (-32000...-32099) because all errors look identical. This collapses the entire error taxonomy into one bucket, making it impossible for a Claude Code tool-use loop to retry intelligently vs abort.

weaknesses.issue: SendInput focus races - keyboard and mouse ops go to whatever window has focus at delivery time
weaknesses.why: All keyboard calls (type_text, press_key, hotkey, key_down) and mouse click calls use SendInput, which delivers to the thread-input-focused window determined at kernel dispatch time. Between MCP tool calls, the Claude Code terminal process can steal focus back (Windows restores focus to the shell window that launched it, or window activation from prior actions settles asynchronously). The session has no SetForegroundWindow+verify-focus step before any keyboard delivery. ghost_focus_window (tree.rs:279-289) calls SetForegroundWindow but does not wait for the WM_SETFOCUS message to arrive or for GetForegroundWindow to confirm the HWND matches before returning. navigate_and_wait (session.rs:517-523) calls focus_window then immediately hotkey Ctrl+L - zero inter-call delay. This is the primary cause of 'failing across the board' in Claude Code tool loops where the terminal steals focus between tool calls.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot always returns full-screen PNG - a ~1-5MB base64 blob every call
weaknesses.why: The ghost_screenshot handler (main.rs:169-172) calls capture_screen() which DXGI-captures the full primary monitor and PNG-encodes it without any downscaling or JPEG option. A 2560x1440 screen produces a ~3-5MB PNG, which base64-encodes to ~4-7MB. This is delivered inline in the JSON-RPC result body, transferred on stdout, and consumed as tool content in the Claude Code context window. At 200K token contexts with ~0.75 byte/token this exhausts roughly 2-4% of context per screenshot. The schema description warns to prefer ghost_screenshot_region, but agents in a tool loop will often reach for the simplest tool. The tool should at minimum default to foreground-window JPEG rather than full-screen PNG.

weaknesses.issue: wait_for_idle hashes the full-screen PNG (lossless, then Blake3) instead of the downsampled perceptual hash it claims to use
weaknesses.why: idle.rs:wait_stable (line 71-83) calls capture_screen() which returns a full PNG blob, then passes the ENTIRE PNG bytes to hash_frame() (which is just blake3::hash). The downsample_to_4x4() function exists and is correct but is NEVER called - wait_stable hashes raw PNG bytes instead. This has two consequences: (1) any PNG encoder non-determinism (e.g. timestamp metadata fields in certain builds of the image crate) will cause every frame to appear 'changed', making wait_for_idle never settle. (2) Even when working, capturing + PNG-encoding a full screen 60 times per second (16ms sleep) to hash it is ~10x more CPU/IO work than needed. The correct fix is to call capture_screen_full_rgba(), downsample_to_4x4(), and hash that.

weaknesses.issue: UIA tree.rs is initialized with COINIT_MULTITHREADED but IUIAutomation is STA-only
weaknesses.why: uia/mod.rs:init_com() (line 18-21) calls CoInitializeEx with COINIT_MULTITHREADED. IUIAutomation (CUIAutomation8) is an in-process COM object that requires STA apartment threading - calling it from an MTA apartment is formally undefined behavior and causes intermittent access violation panics and stale/wrong element data in practice. The sta_pool.rs correctly initializes its workers with COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED, but UiaTree::new() in session.rs:51 creates the IUIAutomation on the tokio thread pool (MTA), and all the find_by_name_fast / describe_screen / etc calls execute COM calls on whatever tokio worker thread handles the async task - none of which have STA affinity. This is a latent race that manifests as random element-not-found failures, UIA cache corruption, and access violations under load.

weaknesses.issue: parse_by() accepts only name or role, not both simultaneously - no compound locator
weaknesses.why: parse_by() (main.rs:819-827) returns the first of name or role it finds. An agent cannot say 'find the button named Save' - it must pick one. In UIs with multiple 'Save' elements of different roles (e.g. Save button vs Save menuitem) or with common role matches (multiple 'button' elements), the agent is forced to either retry with progressively more specific queries or fall back to coordinate-based clicks. The locator system is also missing: CSS-selector style, automation-id, class-name, or XPath-equivalent. For complex apps this means constant fallback to ghost_locate_by_description (expensive API call) for elements that are perfectly accessible via UIA but not matchable by single name/role.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot returns base64 as a JSON string key, not an MCP image content block
weaknesses.why: MCP 2024-11-05 defines a dedicated 'image' content type: {type:'image', data:base64, mimeType:'image/png'}. Ghost returns {png_base64: '...'} (main.rs:171) or {jpeg_base64: '...'} (main.rs:191) - plain JSON string values. Claude Code's tool result handling can render image content blocks as actual images in the conversation, enabling the model to visually inspect the screenshot. With the current plain-string encoding the model receives the raw base64 text, consuming enormous context but getting no actual vision capability from it. This is both a DX issue (the agent cannot 'see' the screenshot without also calling locate_by_description) and a context-bloat issue (base64 string in context vs image block that Claude can process without token cost).

weaknesses.issue: ghost_wait is an explicit sleep tool with no upper-bound guard, and agents over-use it
weaknesses.why: ghost_wait (main.rs:296-299) accepts any ms value. The schema has minimum:0 but no maximum. Agents in a Claude Code loop routinely insert ghost_wait(2000) or ghost_wait(5000) as a defensive wait after any action, turning a potentially event-driven sequence into a fixed-delay polling loop. The real fix is ghost_wait_for_event / ghost_wait_for_idle, but agents choose the simpler tool. There is no per-call cap (e.g. 10 seconds max) to protect against agents issuing ghost_wait(60000) and hanging the tool loop. More importantly, the schema description for ghost_wait does not steer agents toward the event-driven alternatives.

weaknesses.issue: Tool count sprawl - 47 tools visible to Claude Code on every tool-list call
weaknesses.why: tools/list returns all 47 tools unconditionally. Claude Code's tool-selection pass ingests every tool description into the model's context for each turn. At ~50-100 tokens per tool schema, this is 2350-4700 tokens of tool overhead per turn. Several tools overlap in intent: ghost_find + ghost_click + ghost_click_at + ghost_click_by_description + ghost_click_text_local + ghost_click_background represent 5 different click verbs - an agent must reason about which to choose every time. ghost_describe_screen and ghost_describe_screen_fast serve the same purpose. ghost_wait and ghost_wait_for_event + ghost_wait_for_idle + ghost_wait_until are four wait mechanisms. This choice paralysis directly contributes to agent reliability failures as the model picks the suboptimal tool.

weaknesses.issue: Notification messages (no id) are silently executed but their result is discarded
weaknesses.why: main.rs:87-89 - when req.id is None (notification), handle() is called and awaited but the result is completely discarded. If the notification is 'initialized' (the post-handshake notification) this is fine. But any future notification that modifies session state and returns an error will fail silently with no tracing log. A single tracing::debug or tracing::error for the Err case should be added.

weaknesses.issue: base64_encode is a custom implementation duplicated in main.rs and vision.rs
weaknesses.why: The same 20-line base64 encoding function (using a const TABLE) appears identically in ghost-mcp/src/main.rs:829-842 and ghost-session/src/vision.rs:373-386. This violates DRY, adds maintenance surface (a bug fix in one copy won't propagate), and wastes compile time. The workspace already has a base64 dependency path available through image crate internals; alternatively a one-liner via the base64 workspace crate would eliminate both copies.

weaknesses.issue: DXGI DuplicateOutput is called fresh on every screenshot - adds ~5-20ms overhead per capture
weaknesses.why: capture_rgba() in screen.rs:158-212 calls s.output1.DuplicateOutput() at line 164 on every invocation. DuplicateOutput creates a new IDXGIOutputDuplication session, allocates a frame buffer, and returns the latest frame. The D3D11 device and context are correctly cached in CAPTURE_STATE, but the duplication session is not. Re-creating it per call adds 5-20ms of COM/GPU setup overhead and can cause DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST races on rapid sequential captures (wait_for_idle polls at 16ms intervals). The duplication session should be cached in CachedCapture and re-opened only on DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add 'env' block to the ghost MCP server registration in settings.json with NVIDIA_API_KEY (or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) so vision tools actually work when ghost-mcp is spawned by Claude Code
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes the silent failure of ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, and ghost_type_by_description - the three highest-intelligence tools in the toolset. Zero code changes required, one settings.json edit.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a focus-confirm loop in focus_window (tree.rs) and in navigate_and_wait (session.rs): after SetForegroundWindow, poll GetForegroundWindow in a tight loop (max 500ms, 10ms backoff) until the HWND matches, then add a fixed 50ms settle delay before any SendInput call
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the primary keyboard focus race. Claude Code launches each tool call from the terminal process; Windows returns focus to the terminal between calls. A focus-confirmed wait before every SendInput path would make ghost_type, ghost_press, ghost_hotkey, and ghost_navigate_and_wait reliable in a tool loop.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Fix wait_for_idle to use capture_screen_full_rgba() + downsample_to_4x4() + hash_frame() instead of capture_screen() (which PNG-encodes before hashing). The downsample_to_4x4 function already exists at idle.rs:15 but is never called
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes ghost_wait_for_idle actually settle reliably (eliminates PNG encoder non-determinism from the hash), reduces CPU/IO by ~10x per poll cycle, and removes a 60Hz full PNG capture loop from the hot path.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Move UIA tree operations to the existing STA pool (sta_pool.rs) instead of executing them on tokio MTA threads. UiaTree::new() should store an Arc<StaPool> and all find/describe calls should submit closures to it. init_com() in session.rs should be removed or changed to call CoInitializeEx(COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED) only on the STA workers
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes the STA/MTA apartment mismatch that causes intermittent COM access violations, stale UIA element data, and random element-not-found failures under load. The STA pool with circuit breaker already exists - it just needs to be wired into the tree and session code.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Return MCP image content blocks from ghost_screenshot and ghost_screenshot_region instead of raw base64 strings. Return {type:'image', data: base64, mimeType:'image/png'} (or image/jpeg) wrapped in the standard MCP content array format
improvement_opportunities.impact: Lets Claude Code render screenshots as visual image content that the model can actually see, rather than injecting a massive base64 string into the text context. This unlocks the visual inspection loop: take screenshot, see it, act on it - without needing to call locate_by_description.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Change ghost_screenshot to default to foreground-window JPEG (max_dim=768, jpeg_quality=75) instead of full-screen PNG, or deprecate it entirely in favor of ghost_screenshot_region with sensible defaults surfaced prominently in the schema description
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces screenshot context cost from ~4-7MB base64 (~5M tokens equivalent) to ~40-100KB base64 for the common case. At 47 tools the agents already have context pressure; every full-screen PNG worsens it.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Cache the IDXGIOutputDuplication session in CachedCapture (screen.rs). Reopen it only on DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST (e.g. display mode change, RDP reconnect). This removes DuplicateOutput() from the hot path of every screenshot and OCR call
improvement_opportunities.impact: Saves 5-20ms per screen capture call and eliminates DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST races during rapid sequential captures (wait_for_idle at 16ms, OCR polling). Critical path for wait_for_idle convergence speed.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Collapse the 5 click variants and 3 describe variants and 4 wait variants into a unified smart tool: ghost_click({target: name|role|description|coords|text}, strategy?: 'uia'|'vision'|'ocr'|'coord') that auto-selects the cheapest strategy. Similarly ghost_wait({until: 'event'|'idle'|'text'|'condition', ...}). Reduce from 47 to ~20 tools
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces tool-selection paralysis (the agent no longer needs to choose among 5 click verbs), cuts per-turn tool schema overhead from ~4700 to ~2000 tokens, and makes the agent default to the correct strategy (UIA first, OCR fallback, vision last) without needing to reason about it explicitly.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a 'required_env' field to the tools_schema() for vision tools (ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description) that lists NVIDIA_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the description, and add a runtime check at session initialization that warns via tracing::warn! if neither key is set
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes misconfiguration immediately visible in Claude Code's MCP server logs instead of silently failing the first time a vision tool is called mid-task. Agents will also see the key requirement in the tool description and can warn the user upfront.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add JSON-RPC 2.0 compliant error codes to all error responses: parse error (-32700), invalid request (-32600), method not found (-32601), invalid params (-32602), and application errors (-32000). Add a code field to the McpResponse error object
improvement_opportunities.impact: Enables Claude Code and any MCP-compliant client to distinguish retryable errors from fatal ones. Method-not-found can be surfaced to the user directly; invalid-params errors can trigger schema re-read; application errors can be retried. Currently all errors look identical.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Extend parse_by() to support compound locators: {name, role} (AND), {automation_id}, and {index} (nth match). Add these as optional fields to the inputSchema of ghost_find, ghost_click, ghost_type, ghost_get_text, and ghost_click_and_wait_for_text
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the most common reason agents fall back to ghost_locate_by_description: UIA finds multiple elements with the same name but different roles. Compound name+role matching makes ghost_find reliable for apps with repeated element names (e.g. multiple 'Close' buttons at different roles: button vs menuitem).

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a ghost_focus_and_type composite tool that atomically: focus window, verify focus, then type text. Accept a window param. This covers the most common Claude Code failure pattern: type goes to the wrong window because focus was not confirmed
improvement_opportunities.impact: Single highest-ROI tool addition for reliability in Claude Code loops. Replaces the pattern ghost_focus_window + ghost_wait(500) + ghost_type which is what agents currently emit to work around focus races.

audit.purpose: ghost-cache provides two subsystems: (1) UiaCache - an in-memory, event-driven mirror of the UI Automation element tree with 8-slot snapshot history and structural delta API, and (2) LocatorStore - a SQLite-backed cross-run persistent cache mapping (app_id, window_class, role, name) tuples to screen rects with blake3 checksums for invalidation. The design intent is to let ghost-session skip live UIA walks on repeated element lookups and serve incremental describe_screen_delta payloads to Claude-Code tool loops without full desktop rescans.
strengths: LocatorStore schema is production-ready: WAL mode, mmap_size 256MB, synchronous=NORMAL, schema version gating, and a UNIQUE constraint that enables upsert semantics correctly.
UiaCache snapshot diff algorithm (uia_mirror.rs:70-102) is correct and O(n) via two HashMaps; it properly classifies added/removed/updated by runtime_id and ax_checksum.
blake3 truncated to [u8;16] for checksumming is a sound tradeoff - fast, collision-resistant, compact for SQLite BLOB storage.
8-slot ring buffer for snapshot history with proper front-eviction (uia_mirror.rs:175-181) is a good bounded-memory design.
Unit test coverage for LocatorStore is thorough: schema version, upsert-then-lookup, stale checksum eviction, hit_count increment, and restart survival are all tested.
CacheStats tracking (mutations_applied, snapshots_served, deltas_served, history_hits, history_misses) gives good observability when the cache is actually populated.
The chaos feature flag design (chaos_drop_events, test-hooks feature gate) is the right pattern for failure injection without impacting release builds.

weaknesses.issue: UiaCache is never populated from real UIA walks - apply_snapshot has no caller
weaknesses.why: GhostSession constructs UiaCache (session.rs:55) and exposes apply_snapshot (session.rs:125-127), but no code path in ghost-mcp or ghost-session ever calls apply_snapshot with real data. ghost_describe_screen_delta always reads from an empty cache: seq stays at 0, every call returns either an empty delta (since_seq==0) or a full snapshot of zero nodes. The tool is effectively a no-op that returns garbage.

weaknesses.issue: LocatorStore is constructed nowhere and never used in production
weaknesses.why: LocatorStore::open() is never called anywhere outside tests. GhostSession has no LocatorStore field. No find() or fill_form() path consults the store before doing a live UIA walk. The entire SQLite-backed cross-run cache is dead code. The CHANGELOG explicitly defers the read path (v0.5 'Deferred from v0.5' note).

weaknesses.issue: NVIDIA_API_KEY (and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) absent from MCP registration env block
weaknesses.why: Both claude_desktop_config.json and ~/.claude/mcp.json register ghost-mcp with no env:{} block. vision.rs:pick_provider() calls std::env::var('NVIDIA_API_KEY') which returns Err at runtime, causing every ghost_locate_by_description / ghost_click_by_description / ghost_type_by_description call to fail immediately with GhostError::Config. The vision subsystem is fully built but permanently broken in the deployed config.

weaknesses.issue: wait_until condition operates on cache_seq which never advances
weaknesses.why: session.rs:95-98 exposes only {'cache_seq': self.cache.seq()} to JSONLogic conditions in wait_until. Because UiaCache is never populated, seq stays at 0 permanently. Any condition that checks for a seq change (the primary use case for wait_until) will either never fire or fire immediately on seq==0 checks, depending on logic. The tool is semantically broken as a screen-state gate.

weaknesses.issue: EventBus only fires on EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND - within-window changes are invisible
weaknesses.why: event_bus.rs:99-101 hooks exactly one Windows event code. When a button appears, text changes, or a dialog opens within the same window, the seq does not advance. Backoff loops in find(), click_and_wait_for_text(), and click_text_local() wait the full backoff interval (up to 150ms) before re-polling, even when the target just appeared. This is the dominant latency source in fast agent loops.

weaknesses.issue: upsert() uses two round-trips under one lock to obtain the row ID instead of last_insert_rowid()
weaknesses.why: locator_store.rs:120-128 runs a second SELECT query after INSERT...ON CONFLICT to retrieve the id. rusqlite::Connection::last_insert_rowid() returns the correct rowid after both INSERT and the conflict-resolution UPDATE branch in SQLite, making the second round-trip redundant. Under lock contention this doubles the critical section.

weaknesses.issue: Mutex<Connection> blocks all readers during writes - no read parallelism despite WAL mode
weaknesses.why: WAL mode allows concurrent readers and one writer at the OS level, but LocatorStore wraps the single connection in Mutex, serializing every lookup and upsert. In a busy agent loop doing parallel fill_form(), all concurrent find() calls that hit the store will queue behind each other.

weaknesses.issue: snapshot() ignores the window and since_seq parameters entirely
weaknesses.why: uia_mirror.rs:138-141: snapshot(_window, _since_seq) discards both parameters with underscore prefixes and always returns the full current snapshot. The since_seq semantics (which callers would expect to mean 'only give me data newer than this') are silently ignored, leading to potential over-delivery when the cache is eventually populated.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Wire apply_snapshot into describe_screen_fast: after each successful describe_screen_fast() call in session.rs, build a Snapshot from the returned ElementDescriptors and call self.cache.apply_snapshot(snap). This makes ghost_describe_screen_delta immediately useful - the next call returns only the diff. Zero new dependencies required.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Turns ghost_describe_screen_delta from a broken no-op into a working incremental perception tool. Reduces MCP payload size by 80-95% on subsequent calls in stable screens, directly cutting Claude context consumption per agent loop turn.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add NVIDIA_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the ghost MCP registration env block in both claude_desktop_config.json and ~/.claude/mcp.json. Add a startup validation in ghost-mcp main() that calls pick_provider() and logs a warning to stderr if no vision key is found.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Unblocks all five vision tools (ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description, ghost_find_text_local via vision fallback, ghost_click_text_local). Currently 100% of vision-based locator calls fail silently.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Construct LocatorStore in GhostSession::new() (store in a user-profile cache dir like %LOCALAPPDATA%/ghost/locators.db) and consult it at the start of find(). On hit with matching checksum, skip the UIA walk and click the cached rect directly. On miss or checksum mismatch, do the walk, then upsert. Fix last_insert_rowid() while doing this.
improvement_opportunities.impact: The primary stated goal of the crate. On repeated automation of the same app (e.g. every Claude Code loop iteration targeting the same browser controls), UIA walk cost drops to a SQLite lookup (~0.1ms) instead of 10-500ms. Makes the most common hot path effectively free.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Subscribe to UIA structure-changed events via IUIAutomation::AddStructureChangedEventHandler in addition to EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND. The CHANGELOG notes this needs raw VTABLE-level COM; alternatively, add a lightweight polling path that runs find_by_name_fast on a background timer and calls EventBus::bump() on change, bridging the gap until COM handlers are wired.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Closes the latency gap for within-window state changes. Currently find() and click_and_wait_for_text() wait up to 150ms after a within-window change; with this wired the backoff wakeup fires within 1-5ms of the DOM update.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a focus-before-type guard in type_text() and press_key(): call SetForegroundWindow to the target HWND obtained during find() immediately before dispatching SendInput events. Store the HWND on GhostElement. If focus cannot be confirmed (e.g. another window is foreground), return a FocusLost error instead of silently misrouting keystrokes.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Directly addresses the known terminal-steals-focus race in Claude Code tool loops. SendInput currently goes to whatever window happens to be focused at delivery time. With an HWND-pinned focus assertion, type_text and hotkey become deterministic even when the Claude Code terminal competes for foreground.

improvement_opportunities.idea: In ghost-mcp, replace the ghost_screenshot response field png_base64 with a ghost_screenshot_region call (foreground rect, max_dim=768, jpeg_quality=75) as the default, returning jpeg_base64 + size_bytes. Keep the full-PNG path only when rect=null AND format=png are explicitly requested. Update the tool schema accordingly.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Full-screen PNG is the default today and the primary source of oversized base64 payloads in Claude context (can exceed 500KB encoded). JPEG q75 at 768px longest edge produces 15-80KB typical. This is a 10-30x context size reduction per screenshot call.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Replace the Mutex<Connection> in LocatorStore with a r2d2 or deadpool connection pool (2-4 connections), using WAL mode which already supports concurrent readers. Reads (lookup) get a pool connection immediately; writes (upsert, evict) serialize only against each other.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates read-write contention in parallel ghost_fill_form or ghost_batch_actions scenarios where multiple find() calls resolve simultaneously. Latency improvement depends on parallelism, but the correctness improvement (no reader blocking on unrelated writes) is unconditional.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Expose cache_seq in MCP ghost_event_seq response alongside the WinEvent seq, and add it to ghost_describe_screen_delta output. Add a ghost_snapshot tool that both populates the cache (calls describe_screen_fast + apply_snapshot) and returns the seq, giving Claude Code a single call to 'lock in' the current screen state before a batch action.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Enables Claude Code agent loops to do: ghost_snapshot -> record seq -> batch actions -> ghost_describe_screen_delta(since_seq) to see exactly what changed. This is the core perception-action-perception loop needed for reliable autonomous desktop automation.

audit.purpose: Intent compiler, JSONLogic condition evaluator, and FSM executor for the Ghost desktop-automation framework. Provides a JSON-driven step-list abstraction (Op enum) compiled at the MCP boundary, then executed by FsmExecutor via an OpsDispatcher trait that bridges to GhostSession primitives. Used exclusively through the ghost_execute_intent MCP tool, which compiles and runs the intent against a live session.
strengths: Compile-time JSONLogic validation (jsonlogic::validate) catches malformed abort_if/retry_if expressions before execution, preventing runtime surprises.

FsmExecutor is purely synchronous over a trait object (OpsDispatcher), making it unit-testable without any Win32 surface; the test suite covers happy-path, abort, retry cap, timeout, and failure paths.
Exponential retry backoff (50ms * 2^attempt, capped at 32x) is correctly capped to 3 attempts, preventing indefinite runaway in retry_if loops.
Dual timeout check (outer per-op and inner deadline guard) ensures the global max_duration_ms is respected even inside long-polling ops like WaitForText.
The JSONLogic evaluator is intentionally minimal and covers exactly the subset needed (==, !=, <, <=, >, >=, &&, ||, !, in, contains, var) — no over-engineered operator surface.
benchmark harness exists (benches/hot_paths.rs) with documented budget targets, enabling regression detection on the hot compile+eval path.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_execute_intent is a dead-end abstraction with no feedback loop
weaknesses.why: session.rs:602-607 shows execute_intent compiles the intent then runs FsmExecutor. The Op::Screenshot variant at session.rs:686-688 captures a full PNG and discards the bytes entirely — the result is never returned to the caller or the MCP client. Claude cannot see what happened, cannot course-correct, and gets no screenshot for the next decision. This makes ghost_execute_intent effectively blind for any visual-verification step. The IntentResult struct (executor.rs:45-50) also carries no screenshot or UIA snapshot of the final state.

weaknesses.issue: Op::Navigate hard-codes Edge/Chrome/Firefox heuristic with no configurability
weaknesses.why: session.rs:670-679: Navigate scans list_windows() for a window whose title contains 'Edge', 'Chrome', or 'Firefox'. Any other browser (Brave, Arc, Opera, Vivaldi, custom Electron shell) returns 'no browser window'. There is no way to pass a window hint via the Op enum. This is a silent hard failure with no user guidance in the error message about how to fix it.

weaknesses.issue: Vision not wired in the direct ghost-mcp registration env block
weaknesses.why: vision.rs:96-114 picks a provider by checking NVIDIA_API_KEY then ANTHROPIC_API_KEY at runtime. The ghost-mcp binary receives its environment from the MCP host's env block. The comet-mcp wrapper is the only path where NVIDIA_API_KEY is injected; in a direct ghost registration (typical Claude Code setup) neither key is forwarded unless the user explicitly sets them in the MCP env config. The tools ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description all silently fail with a GhostError::Config at call time rather than at startup or tools/list time, giving no diagnostic until the agent tries to use vision.

weaknesses.issue: retry_if evaluates a fixed expression that cannot observe what actually failed
weaknesses.why: executor.rs:117-128: retry_if is a JSONLogic expression evaluated against IntentState, which only exposes last_error (a string) and last_op_index. The test at executor.rs:213 uses {"==":[1,1]} — a literal always-true expression — as the retry condition, meaning the intent will always retry on any failure regardless of cause. In production use there is no structured error code to match against; last_error is a free-form string from whatever the dispatcher returns. This makes retry_if impossible to use reliably for targeted retry (e.g., retry only on element-not-found, abort on focus-stolen).

weaknesses.issue: SendInput focus races: keyboard ops go to whatever window has focus, with no pre-op focus assertion
weaknesses.why: keyboard.rs:50-64 calls SendInput per character with no focus check between the click that should have set focus and the subsequent type_text. In a Claude Code tool loop, the terminal hosting the MCP stdin/stdout loop steals focus back between tool calls (each tool call is a round-trip; Windows delivers WM_ACTIVATE to the terminal when MCP completes). type_text then delivers keystrokes to the terminal. The Op::Type dispatcher (session.rs:624-627) does el.click() then el.type_text() in the same dispatch call, which is somewhat safer, but Op::Press and Op::Hotkey (session.rs:629-637) issue SendInput with no prior focus assertion at all — they blindly send to the foreground window.

weaknesses.issue: EventBus only fires on EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND; misses element changes within a focused window
weaknesses.why: event_bus.rs:99-107: the WinEventHook covers only EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND. Structural UIA changes (buttons appearing/disappearing, edit fields enabling, loading spinners) do not bump the bus seq. wait_for_change therefore falls through to its backoff timer rather than waking on the relevant state change.
