The WaitForText op in executor.rs uses bus.wait_for_change at lines 655-658 but the bus will not fire until the user alt-tabs or another window pops. This means WaitForText degrades to timed-poll in practice.

weaknesses.issue: abort_if and retry_if share a single IntentState scope — abort evaluation swallows JSONLogic eval errors
weaknesses.why: executor.rs:104-116: abort_if is evaluated with jsonlogic::eval; if eval itself errors (e.g., var references a path not present in state), the error is silently ignored (if let Ok(v) = ...) and the intent continues executing rather than surfacing the misconfiguration. This can mask intent author bugs.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot tool returns full-screen lossless PNG with no size guard
weaknesses.why: main.rs:169-172: ghost_screenshot calls session.screenshot(Region::full()) which calls capture_screen() (full primary monitor, PNG). A 2560x1440 screen produces roughly 2-6MB of PNG, encoded as ~2.7-8MB of base64, sent inline in the MCP JSON response. Claude Code context windows are finite; large screenshots consume tokens rapidly. The tool description does not warn about this. ghost_screenshot_region exists with max_dim and jpeg_quality but ghost_screenshot has no guardrails.

weaknesses.issue: Op::Click and Op::Type use By::Name only; no By::Role or combined locators in the intent schema
weaknesses.why: compiler.rs:11-12: Op::Click { target: String } and Op::Type { target, text } map target directly to By::Name. session.rs:619-627 confirms this. There is no way in the intent JSON schema to click by role, by coordinate, or by description. The intent abstraction therefore cannot express many operations that ghost_click_at, ghost_click_by_description, or role-based finds can do via direct MCP tools, making it less capable than the lower-level API.

weaknesses.issue: OCR run_ocr calls IAsyncOperation::get() on the tokio thread, blocking the async runtime
weaknesses.why: ocr.rs:159-165: block_on_async calls op.get() which is a synchronous blocking wait on a WinRT async operation. This is called from find_text_local, which is called from GhostSession::find_text_local as an async fn, which is awaited on the tokio single-thread runtime. A full-screen OCR pass can take 100-500ms; blocking the runtime thread for this duration prevents all other tokio tasks (including MCP stdin processing) from making progress.

weaknesses.issue: retry cap is hard-coded to 3 with no per-intent configurability
weaknesses.why: executor.rs:123: `attempt < 3` is a literal. There is no max_retries field in CompiledIntent or RawIntent. An agent that submits an intent with retry_if cannot control how many retries are attempted, so the same transient error either exhausts retries too fast (for slow operations) or the cap is never surfaced as a configurable knob.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a FocusWindow op before every Op::Press and Op::Hotkey in SessionOpsDispatcher, or add an optional 'focus' field to Op::Press/Op::Hotkey in the compiler. Before issuing SendInput, assert GetForegroundWindow matches the intended target and retry the focus call if not. This closes the SendInput focus-race that causes keystrokes to land in the terminal.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the most common 'failing across the board' class: keyboard input going to the wrong window in a Claude Code agent loop.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Return the final IntentResult enriched with an optional foreground-window screenshot and describe_screen_fast snapshot. Add screenshot_b64: Option<String> and elements: Option<Vec<ElementDescriptor>> to IntentResult, populated at the end of FsmExecutor::run (or on failure). This gives Claude a closed-loop signal: it can see what the screen looks like after the intent completed without a separate tool call.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Transforms ghost_execute_intent from a fire-and-hope primitive into a closed-loop action that supports visual verification, cutting the tool-call round-trips needed for confirmation by 1-2 per intent.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Extend Op with Coordinate variants (ClickAt { x, y }, TypeAt { x, y, text }) and a ByDescription variant (ClickByDescription { description, text }) so the intent schema is as expressive as the direct MCP tools. Currently Op::Click/Type are name-only which means agents must fall back to individual MCP calls for coordinate-based or vision-based actions.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the capability gap between ghost_execute_intent and the individual ghost_click_at / ghost_click_by_description tools, enabling the agent to express full automation sequences as a single intent.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Subscribe the EventBus to UIA EVENT_OBJECT_STATECHANGE, EVENT_OBJECT_NAMECHANGE, and EVENT_SYSTEM_ALERT in addition to EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND. This gives WaitForText and WaitForIdle real event-driven wakeups instead of falling back to timed polling. Use IUIAutomation::AddStructureChangedEventHandler scoped to the foreground window.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Converts WaitForText from a 25-150ms poll to a sub-5ms event-driven wakeup, reducing intent execution time for multi-step sequences by 50-200ms per wait step.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Move OCR (ocr.rs block_on_async) off the tokio thread with tokio::task::spawn_blocking. The current IAsyncOperation::get() blocks the async runtime for 100-500ms per OCR call. spawn_blocking routes it to the blocking thread pool, keeping the MCP stdin loop responsive during vision and OCR calls.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates MCP responsiveness stalls during ghost_find_text_local and ghost_click_text_local calls; prevents dropped or delayed tool responses when OCR is running.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add startup-time validation of ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and NVIDIA_API_KEY in GhostSession::new() and include their presence/absence in the tools/list capabilities response. If neither key is set, mark ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description as unavailable with an explanatory note rather than silently failing at call time.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Fixes the 'vision not wired' silent failure: agents discover at session init whether vision is available and can choose fallback strategies (UIA, OCR) rather than burning a tool call to get a Config error.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add max_retries: u32 (default 3) to RawIntent/CompiledIntent and thread it through FsmExecutor::run. Expose it in the ghost_execute_intent inputSchema. Also add an error_code field to IntentError so retry_if can match on structured codes (ElementNotFound, Timeout, FocusLost) rather than string-matching last_error.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Enables correct retry policies: retry ElementNotFound up to 5 times, abort immediately on FocusLost, retry Timeout once with a longer backoff. Currently all retries are indistinguishable and always-on with no control.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Remove ghost_screenshot from the tools/list or add a deprecation note directing agents to ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true, max_dim=768, jpeg_quality=75. Add a size_bytes field to the ghost_screenshot response so the agent can see how large it was. The full-screen lossless PNG is 10-50x larger than a compressed foreground crop and provides no additional information for most agent tasks.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces per-screenshot token cost from ~5000-20000 tokens (base64 PNG) to ~300-800 tokens (JPEG foreground crop at 768px), directly addressing the 'base64 screenshots too big for context' pain point.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a pre-op focus_window assertion to Op::Hotkey and Op::Press in SessionOpsDispatcher: resolve the intended window from Op context (or from a new optional window field), call focus_window before SendInput, and verify GetForegroundWindow matches before dispatching the key events. This is separate from the per-step FocusWindow op because it can be done automatically without requiring the intent author to insert explicit focus steps.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Prevents the terminal focus-steal race without requiring intent authors to manually insert FocusWindow ops before every key action, reducing intent verbosity and preventing silent misdirected keystrokes.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Cache the compiled CompiledIntent in the MCP layer keyed by a hash of the intent JSON. IntentCompiler::compile does serde_json deserialization and JSONLogic validation on every ghost_execute_intent call. For repeated agent loops that submit the same intent structure, this is wasted work. A small LRU cache (e.g., 32 entries) keyed by the JSON string's xxHash would skip recompilation on cache hit.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Cuts intent compilation overhead (currently ~10-40us per compile per the bench budget) to near-zero for repeated intents in tight agent loops, reducing per-tool-call latency.

audit.purpose: ghost-http is a localhost-only REST/HTTP facade (Axum on 127.0.0.1:7878) that re-exposes the same GhostSession automation primitives (click, type, screenshot, describe, etc.) that ghost-mcp already provides over stdio JSON-RPC. Its existence is justified for two narrow cases: (1) languages or tools that cannot spawn a stdio MCP child process but can do HTTP (e.g., the Python ghost-agent/agent.py loop); (2) quick curl-based debugging. For the Claude Code MCP tool loop the actual server in use is ghost-mcp, not ghost-http. ghost-http is therefore an auxiliary binary sharing the same ghost-session and ghost-core underpinnings.
strengths: Correct COM thread affinity design: GhostSession is !Send (COM), so ghost-http dedicates a real OS thread with its own Tokio single-threaded runtime (spawn_session_thread) and serialises all requests via an mpsc channel actor, avoiding UB (main.rs:55-161).

All HTTP endpoints fully handled: 15 routes cover every public GhostSession operation, with appropriate GET vs POST split, JSON error bodies, and correct HTTP status codes (422 for automation failures, 500 for channel death).
tower-http CorsLayer::permissive() is appropriate here because the server only binds to 127.0.0.1 — not a public interface — and local same-machine callers are inherently trusted.
vision.rs has a robust response parser that handles code-fences, prose wrapping, and {found:false} returns, with a hand-rolled base64 encoder that passes RFC 4648 test vectors.

describe_screen_delta and the UIA event bus provide efficient incremental state reads: the agent loop can pass since_seq and get only changed elements rather than re-describing the full desktop on every tool call.
ghost_batch_actions in ghost-mcp allows multiple actions per round-trip, directly attacking the latency of a Claude Code tool loop that otherwise issues one tool call at a time.
Emergency stop (Ctrl+Alt+G) is checked at every entry point across all input primitives; is_stopped() is polled inside type_text per-character (keyboard.rs:52-56), providing a reliable abort.

weaknesses.issue: NVIDIA_API_KEY (and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) are missing from claude_desktop_config.json MCP registration
weaknesses.why: claude_desktop_config.json has no 'env' key for ghost-mcp (confirmed at C:/Users/Krist/AppData/Roaming/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json). vision.rs:pick_provider() reads NVIDIA_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the process environment. When Claude Desktop spawns ghost-mcp.exe as an MCP child process, it inherits the user environment only if Claude Desktop itself was launched from a shell that had those vars set. In practice, GUI-launched Claude Desktop does NOT inherit terminal-set env vars on Windows. Result: every call to ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description returns GhostError::Config('no vision provider configured') silently as an MCP error string, with no indication in the Claude Code tool output that the root cause is a missing key. Vision is effectively dead in the MCP path.

weaknesses.issue: SendInput keyboard/mouse races: Claude Code terminal steals focus between MCP tool calls
weaknesses.why: keyboard.rs:type_text and press_key call SendInput() which delivers to whatever window holds the thread input queue at call time. Between each MCP tool call, Claude Desktop's own terminal/UI can re-acquire focus (Windows foreground lock is released when the MCP response JSON is sent and the calling thread unblocks). The session thread then wins the next key event lottery against whichever window happened to be foregrounded. There is no pre-type focus assertion in h_type (main.rs:204-207): it calls session.find(by) then element.type_text() with no SetForegroundWindow in between. fill_form in session.rs:570-585 has the same gap — it clicks an element then immediately types without confirming focus transfer. The backoff in find() waits for UIA events, not for the input queue to settle.

weaknesses.issue: ghost-http exposes no authentication and binds to 127.0.0.1 but has no secret/token check
weaknesses.why: Any process on the local machine can POST /click, /type, /run with arbitrary payloads. On a developer workstation this is tolerable, but /run accepts a raw JSON intent string (h_run at main.rs:243-245) that is passed directly to execute_intent, which compiles and runs an FSM with arbitrary steps including Navigate{url} (which calls navigate_and_wait and can open arbitrary URLs in the browser), Launch{exe} (which calls proc_launch with an arbitrary executable path), and hotkey sequences. There is no rate limiting, no origin check (CorsLayer::permissive), and no audit log.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot in both ghost-http and ghost-mcp returns a full-resolution lossless PNG as base64, which is enormous for a Claude Code tool response
weaknesses.why: capture_screen() in session.rs:303-305 captures the primary monitor as raw PNG. At 1920x1080 a typical PNG is 1-3 MB; base64-encoded that is ~4 MB per response. The ghost_screenshot_region tool with JPEG compression exists in ghost-mcp but ghost-http has no equivalent (no /screenshot-region route). The MCP screenshot tool schema (ghost_screenshot) gives no guidance to the agent to use the cheaper ghost_screenshot_region instead, and ghost_screenshot is listed first in tools/list, so the agent will reach for it by default. Each full-PNG screenshot burns ~6000-8000 context tokens and adds noticeable round-trip latency.

weaknesses.issue: mpsc::UnboundedSender in ghost-http allows unbounded queue growth if the session thread stalls
weaknesses.why: spawn_session_thread (main.rs:55-161) uses mpsc::unbounded_channel. If GhostSession::find times out (5 s default) on a slow walk, incoming HTTP requests continue to enqueue Cmd variants with no back-pressure. Under a fast agent loop this can accumulate dozens of pending commands before the timeout fires, then flush them all in sequence, causing phantom actions against whatever UI state exists at flush time. ghost-mcp is not affected (it processes one request at a time over stdio), but ghost-http as an HTTP server is inherently concurrent.

weaknesses.issue: DescribeReq dual-dispatch on since_seq is a silent footgun: omitting since_seq calls full describe, which is slow
weaknesses.why: h_describe (main.rs:230-236) dispatches to Cmd::Describe (full walk) when since_seq is absent, and to Cmd::DescribeDelta when present. The field is Option<u64> and defaults to None if not sent. An agent that forgets to include since_seq gets the full slow describe walk with no warning. The ghost-mcp equivalent (ghost_describe_screen_delta) has the same silent behaviour. The cheap fast path (describe_screen_fast) is a separate endpoint/tool entirely and is not the default.

weaknesses.issue: type_text sends one SendInput syscall per character; for long strings this creates a per-char overhead and a window of focus loss between chars
weaknesses.why: keyboard.rs:type_text (line 50-64) iterates over chars and calls SendInput with a 2-element array per character, checking is_stopped() between each. For a 200-character paste this is 200 SendInput calls. Windows allows batching all character inputs into a single SendInput call (the API accepts a slice), which would be both faster and atomic from the focus-lock perspective. The per-char loop also creates 200 opportunities for a concurrent SetForegroundWindow to steal the queue.

weaknesses.issue: vision.rs builds base64 inline in the request body string for NVIDIA path, allocating a full copy of the JPEG in memory as a String before serialising to JSON
weaknesses.why: locate_via_openai_compat (vision.rs:205-258) calls base64_encode(image_jpeg) to produce a String, then embeds it in serde_json::json!([{...image_url...}]) which allocates another JSON Value tree, then .json(&req) serialises that to bytes a third time. For a 1024-max-dim JPEG at quality 80 (~50-150 KB), the JPEG is held in three copies simultaneously. The Anthropic path has the same pattern. Using reqwest's streaming body or serde's serialize_with to write base64 directly to the JSON encoder would avoid the intermediate allocations.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add 'env': {'NVIDIA_API_KEY': '...', 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY': '...'} to the ghost-mcp entry in claude_desktop_config.json, or document that users must set these in the system environment and restart Claude Desktop (not just a terminal session). Add a startup check in ghost-mcp main() that prints a tracing::warn if neither key is set, so the MCP error surface makes the root cause visible.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Unblocks vision entirely in the MCP tool loop. Currently every ghost_locate_by_description call silently fails.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a focus-assert before every SendInput-based action: in session.rs, after session.find() resolves an element, call SetForegroundWindow on the element's parent HWND and use AttachThreadInput to flush the input queue before type_text or press_key. For coordinate-based actions (click_at, hotkey), call SetForegroundWindow on the topmost window at that coordinate. Add a short WaitForInputIdle (or a 50ms DXGI idle) after SetForegroundWindow to let the window activate before injecting keys.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the primary 'terminal steals focus between tool calls' failure mode. This is the single biggest source of wrong-target keystrokes in the agent loop.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Batch all characters in type_text into a single SendInput call by pre-building the full Vec<INPUT> and calling SendInput once. This makes the entire string atomic from the Win32 input queue perspective: no window switch can interleave between characters. Keep the is_stopped() check only at entry. For very long strings (>256 chars) split into 256-char batches to stay within SendInput's practical limits.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates per-character focus races, reduces syscall overhead from O(n) to O(1) for typing, and makes clipboard-paste unnecessary as a workaround for long text.

improvement_opportunities.idea: In ghost-mcp's tools/list, demote ghost_screenshot to a secondary tool and promote ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true + max_dim=768 + jpeg_quality=75 as the recommended default in its description. Add a reciprocal /screenshot-region endpoint to ghost-http. Add a note to ghost_screenshot's description: 'Returns full-resolution PNG (~1-4MB base64). Prefer ghost_screenshot_region for agent loops.'
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces per-screenshot context consumption by 10-50x, directly improving agent loop throughput and reducing Claude context window pressure.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Replace mpsc::unbounded_channel in ghost-http with mpsc::channel(4) (bounded). When the channel is full, return HTTP 503 with a Retry-After header instead of silently queuing. This gives the agent caller back-pressure awareness and prevents phantom-action storms when the session thread stalls on a slow UIA walk.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Prevents unbounded action queuing that causes incorrect automation sequences when the target app is slow.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a GHOST_HTTP_TOKEN environment variable check in ghost-http: if set, require an Authorization: Bearer <token> header on all non-health routes (405 otherwise). Make the token auto-generated on first launch and printed to stderr. This gates /run's arbitrary intent execution and /launch's arbitrary process spawning against local privilege escalation.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Closes the localhost-only-but-unauthenticated attack surface for /run and /launch, which can execute arbitrary exes and open arbitrary URLs.

improvement_opportunities.idea: In the agent loop (agent.py and ghost-mcp), add a ghost_focus_window call as the first step of every action sequence that targets a specific window, and verify it succeeded before proceeding with keyboard input. Optionally expose a compound 'focus_then_type' batch op in ghost_batch_actions that does focus + optional wait_for_idle + type atomically in one round-trip.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the class of 'typed into wrong window' bugs without requiring OS-level focus locking on the Rust side.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add GHOST_VISION_PROVIDER, GHOST_VISION_MODEL, and GHOST_VISION_BASE_URL to the MCP registration's env block (even as empty strings that fall through to the auto-detect logic), and add a ghost_vision_status tool that reports which provider is active (or why none is configured). This makes the vision subsystem inspectable from within a Claude Code tool loop.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes vision configuration failures diagnosable without looking at OS env vars or Rust tracing logs.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Implement streaming base64 in vision.rs: write the JPEG bytes directly to a base64-encoding wrapper around serde_json's serializer, or at minimum write to a pre-sized String (capacity = (jpeg_len + 2) / 3 * 4) without the intermediate JSON Value allocation. The current flow holds three copies of the JPEG in memory simultaneously.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Halves peak memory allocation per vision call; negligible for small crops but meaningful for full-screen JPEG at max_dim=1024.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a /describe-fast GET endpoint to ghost-http that maps to describe_screen_fast() (foreground-window-only UIA walk, 5-50x faster than full describe). Currently ghost-http's /describe always falls through to the full walk when since_seq is absent, with no fast path exposed over HTTP.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Gives the Python ghost-agent loop a cheap state-read that does not stall for 500ms+ on full desktop walks.

audit.purpose: Single-binary CLI front-end for Ghost Windows desktop automation. Parses subcommands via Clap, constructs a GhostSession, and dispatches to ghost-session primitives (UIA tree walks, SendInput, DXGI screenshot, OCR, vision). Serves as the scripting/testing interface when MCP is not in use. The MCP server (ghost-mcp) is a separate binary in the workspace that speaks stdio JSON-RPC over the same ghost-session library.
strengths: ghost-mcp has 47 well-documented tools covering the full UIA+input surface area with correct JSON-RPC 2.0 framing and per-tool inputSchema definitions.
ghost_batch_actions allows pipelining a whole action sequence in one MCP round-trip, directly addressing the per-call latency problem in agent loops.
Event-driven waits (ghost_wait_for_event, EventBus) eliminate busy polling by subscribing to EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND via SetWinEventHook with a proper message pump thread.
describe_screen_fast scopes the UIA walk to the foreground window subtree, giving 5-50x speedups over the full desktop walk.
screenshot_region with foreground=true + max_dim + jpeg_quality provides correctly-sized, compressed images suitable for vision payloads, and the MCP tool description explains exactly how to use it.
ghost_click_background uses PostMessage-based clicks that avoid focus stealing, which is the right primitive for background automation.
OCR path (Windows.Media.Ocr) is on-device, ~50-200ms, and integrated as ghost_find_text_local / ghost_click_text_local with polling + EventBus backoff.
Vision fallback picks NVIDIA or Anthropic dynamically from env vars, crops to the foreground window, downscales to 1024px, and JPEG-encodes before the API call.
Emergency stop (Ctrl+Alt+G) propagates an atomic STOP_FLAG checked at every SendInput site and every async poll loop iteration.
CLI main.rs is clean, tiny (~220 LOC), and re-uses the same GhostSession API, making the CLI a thin wrapper with no duplicated logic.

weaknesses.issue: CLI completely missing vision, OCR, and composite commands
weaknesses.why: ghost-cli/src/main.rs has no subcommands for locate-by-description, click-by-description, find-text-local, click-text-local, or screenshot-region. All those session methods exist and MCP exposes them, but a scripter or test runner using the CLI binary cannot access them. This is a parity gap that makes the CLI useless for the intelligence/fallback tier.

weaknesses.issue: CLI Serve subcommand is a hard error stub
weaknesses.why: Command::Serve at main.rs:203 immediately returns Err('use ghost-http binary') with no server started. A user who reads --help and runs ghost serve gets a confusing failure rather than a running server or a clear build-time option. This breaks any scripting workflow that tries to use the CLI for HTTP-based access.

weaknesses.issue: SendInput goes to whatever window has focus at call time -- focus races between tool calls are the primary source of automation failures in the Claude Code loop
weaknesses.why: keyboard.rs:type_text (line 50) and press_key (line 67) use raw SendInput with no preceding SetForegroundWindow or AttachThreadInput. Between two MCP tool calls the terminal (Claude Code) can steal focus back, so the next type or press goes to the terminal. ghost_click_at also relies on MOUSEEVENTF_ABSOLUTE which is correct, but the click target must be in the foreground for focus-dependent edits. There is no built-in settle delay or focus-assurance step before any keyboard op. The CLI has the same problem: ghost type ... immediately fires SendInput without confirming focus.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot returns an uncompressed full-screen PNG with no size guard, blowing up context windows
weaknesses.why: session.rs:screenshot (line 303) calls capture_screen() which always encodes the full-resolution primary monitor as PNG. At 1920x1080 lossless PNG this is typically 500KB-2MB of raw JSON base64 (~670KB-2.7MB encoded), consuming a massive fraction of a 200K context. ghost-mcp:ghost_screenshot handler at mcp/main.rs:169 encodes the PNG in base64 and returns it directly with no max_dim or quality limit. The better tool (ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true + max_dim=768 + jpeg_quality=75) exists but is not the default. The MCP description for ghost_screenshot does not warn about size.

weaknesses.issue: Vision is silently broken when NVIDIA_API_KEY is absent from the ghost-mcp process environment
weaknesses.why: vision.rs:pick_provider (line 96) checks env vars GHOST_VISION_PROVIDER, NVIDIA_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the ghost-mcp process. The Claude Code MCP config's 'env' block for the ghost server must include these keys. If they are absent (as noted in the known pain), every call to ghost_locate_by_description / ghost_click_by_description returns GhostError::Config, but this surfaces to the LLM as a generic error string with no actionable hint about which env var to set in the MCP server config specifically. The comet-mcp wrapper sets NVIDIA_API_KEY in its env, but direct ghost MCP registration may not, making vision silently dead.

weaknesses.issue: IdleDetector.wait_stable hashes the full-resolution PNG (not the 4x4 downsample) making it slow and CPU-heavy
weaknesses.why: idle.rs:wait_stable (line 62) calls capture_screen() which returns a full PNG, then hash_frame hashes all bytes of that PNG. The file has downsample_to_4x4 defined but never calls it in wait_stable. At 16ms poll intervals on a 4K screen, this encodes and hashes several MB of PNG per frame for up to timeout_ms. The design comment says 'downsample to 4x4' but the implementation does not do so.

weaknesses.issue: EventBus only fires on EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND; no UIA structure-changed or value-changed events
weaknesses.why: event_bus.rs:pump_thread (line 97) hooks EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND only. So find() and click_and_wait_for_text() will only wake early on a window-focus change, not when a button appears/disappears, a spinner resolves, or a field becomes enabled within the same window. In practice this means the backoff timers degrade to polling (25ms-150ms) for most in-app state changes, making agent loops slower than they should be.

weaknesses.issue: CLI ghost screenshot writes a file, outputs ok+path JSON, but does not print base64 -- unusable in piped scripting without a separate cat step
weaknesses.why: main.rs:143-148: screenshot saves to a path and prints JSON {ok, bytes, path}. For scripting or testing, there is no --stdout flag to emit base64. Every CLI screenshot requires the caller to then read the file, which is a two-step process. MCP returns base64 inline; CLI should offer the same.

weaknesses.issue: collect_interactive hard-caps at 500 elements and depth 50 with no warning emitted
weaknesses.why: tree.rs:193: when either limit is hit the function silently returns Ok(()), so a complex app (VS Code, Office) silently truncates the element list. The caller gets a partial list with no indication that it was cut. The LLM will then attempt to click elements that appear absent.

weaknesses.issue: DXGI capture acquires and releases DuplicateOutput on every capture_rgba call instead of holding the duplication object
weaknesses.why: screen.rs:164: DuplicateOutput is called inside capture_rgba every time. The D3D11 device and output1 are cached (CAPTURE_STATE) but not the IDXGIOutputDuplication. Each call to wait_stable (which polls at 16ms) re-acquires the duplication, adding 5-20ms overhead per frame and risking DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST under sustained load.

weaknesses.issue: parse_by in CLI requires exactly --name OR --role; no --id, no XPath, no automation-id, no combined selector
weaknesses.why: cli/main.rs:212-218: the only two locator axes exposed through the CLI are Name and Role. MCP is identical (parse_by in mcp/main.rs:819). There is no way to scope by window+name simultaneously, use AutomationId (which is stable across locale changes), or combine role+name. This makes Click/Type flaky when two elements share a name.

weaknesses.issue: type_text sends one SendInput call per character in a loop, causing visible typing lag and wasted syscalls
weaknesses.why: keyboard.rs:type_text (line 50) iterates char by char, converts each to a 2-element INPUT array, and calls SendInput. For a 50-char string this is 50 syscalls. All inputs could be batched into a single SendInput call since none of the intermediate state matters.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a ghost_focus_and_type session primitive that calls SetForegroundWindow + AttachThreadInput + a configurable settle delay (default 80ms) before every keyboard SendInput. Expose it in MCP as a replacement for ghost_type and wire focus-assurance into fill_form. This is the single highest-leverage fix for the 'keys go to terminal' race.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the most common class of Claude Code loop failures where typed text goes to the wrong window. Makes ghost_type, ghost_press, ghost_hotkey reliable across tool-call boundaries without requiring the user to manually insert ghost_focus_window before every keyboard action.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Change ghost_screenshot to default to foreground=true + max_dim=768 + jpeg_quality=75, returning jpeg_base64. Keep ghost_screenshot_lossless as the escape hatch for full PNG. Update the MCP tool description to state the output size (typical: 30-80KB) so the LLM understands the tradeoff.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Prevents context overflow on every screenshot call. A 1920x1080 PNG at 1.5MB turns into a 768x432 JPEG at ~35KB -- a 40x reduction. This directly unblocks agent loops that currently stall or fail on context-length limits.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Wire ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (or NVIDIA_API_KEY) into the ghost-mcp Claude Code settings block under the 'env' key, and emit a structured startup diagnostic to stderr listing which vision provider is active (or 'vision disabled: set NVIDIA_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'). Also make ghost_locate_by_description's error message include the exact env var name and where to set it in the MCP config.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Vision tools stop being silently broken. The LLM gets actionable error messages it can relay to the user. Fixes the known pain point where NVIDIA_API_KEY is set only in the comet-mcp wrapper.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Fix IdleDetector.wait_stable to capture raw RGBA (capture_screen_full_rgba), call downsample_to_4x4, then hash_frame the 64-byte thumbnail instead of hashing a full PNG. The infrastructure (hash_frame, downsample_to_4x4) already exists in idle.rs but is unused.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces per-frame work in wait_stable from ~10ms (PNG encode + hash 500KB) to ~1ms (capture + 4x4 average + hash 64 bytes). Makes ghost_wait_for_idle responsive and cheap enough to call after every action.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Subscribe EventBus to UIA structure-changed and focus-changed events via IUIAutomation::AddStructureChangedEventHandler and AddFocusChangedEventHandler on the desktop root, in addition to EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND. Bump seq on each.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes find(), click_and_wait_for_text(), and click_text_local() wake immediately when an element appears rather than waiting up to the backoff ceiling (25-150ms). Removes the largest source of unnecessary latency in multi-step agent loops.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add AutomationId as a third By variant (By::AutomationId) and expose it in both CLI (--automation-id) and MCP (automation_id param in parse_by). AutomationId is locale-stable and developer-assigned, making it a far more reliable selector than name for most enterprise apps.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates element-not-found failures in localized apps and in apps where accessible names vary by version. Directly improves click/type reliability for any structured form automation.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add CLI subcommands for find-text-local, click-text-local, locate-by-description, click-by-description, screenshot-region, wait-for-idle, and batch (accepting a JSON file). This achieves full parity with the MCP surface so that scripted tests can exercise the same paths as the LLM.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Allows testing of the full automation stack without Claude Code. Critical for CI regression testing and for humans debugging automation scripts. Currently the CLI can only exercise ~40% of the session API.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Batch type_text to send all characters in a single SendInput call. Pre-allocate the INPUT vec as text.chars().count() * 2 elements, fill them all, then call SendInput once.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces per-character syscall overhead from O(n) to O(1). For a 100-char paste this is 100x fewer kernel transitions. Eliminates visible character-by-character typing lag in slower apps.

improvement_opportunities.idea: When collect_interactive truncates at the 500-element or depth-50 limit, include a 'truncated: true, total_found: N' field in the MCP response so the LLM knows the list is partial and can narrow the scope with the window parameter or describe_screen_fast.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Prevents the LLM from silently operating on incomplete element data, which causes click failures on elements that appear absent. Turns a silent correctness bug into an observable signal.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Cache IDXGIOutputDuplication inside CachedCapture and reuse it across capture_rgba calls, re-acquiring only on DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST. Add a per-call retry on that error code.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces per-screenshot overhead by 5-20ms (DuplicateOutput acquisition cost). Makes wait_stable and rapid screenshot sequences significantly faster under sustained load.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a ghost_type_safe MCP tool that wraps ghost_focus_window + 80ms settle + ghost_type in a single round-trip, with an optional window_hint parameter. Deprecate bare ghost_type for interactive targets.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Single tool call that is reliably race-free for the most common agent loop pattern: 'click a field, then type text'. Removes the need for the LLM to manually orchestrate focus before every type.

audit.purpose: Ghost MCP server - Windows desktop automation via UIA + SendInput, exposed as a stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 MCP server (ghost-mcp.exe). Drives native Windows UI for Claude Code agent loops. 7 crates: ghost-core (UIA/input/capture/OCR), ghost-session (high-level session API), ghost-mcp (stdio MCP entry point), ghost-intent (FSM executor), ghost-cache (UIA snapshot delta), ghost-cli, ghost-http.
strengths: JSON-RPC 2.0 transport is robust: parse errors return a response and continue the stdio loop; tool errors return error objects without killing the process; notifications are handled correctly with no response emitted.
ghost_batch_actions reduces round-trips for known sequences - agent can submit focus+type+press in one MCP call, shrinking the inter-tool-call window where focus can drift.
Event-driven wait primitives (ghost_wait_for_event, ghost_wait_for_idle) via SetWinEventHook on a dedicated pump thread give sub-5ms wakeup latency instead of sleep loops.
ghost_screenshot_region with foreground=true + max_dim + jpeg_quality provides a correct low-cost vision path (10-50x smaller than full PNG).
Local OCR via Windows.Media.Ocr (ghost_find_text_local, ghost_click_text_local) avoids paid API calls entirely for text-detection waits.
ghost_click_background uses PostMessage WM_LBUTTONDOWN/UP which bypasses focus entirely - the one tool that is focus-safe by design.
Cargo check passes cleanly on the working tree (only warnings, no errors); v0.5.0 source is compilable.

weaknesses.issue: focus_window has no verify-foreground retry - SetForegroundWindow is fire-and-forget
weaknesses.why: focus_window() in tree.rs calls SetForegroundWindow(hwnd) and returns immediately with no confirmation loop. The Win32 API returns a BOOL that is ignored (let _ = SetForegroundWindow(...)). The next tool call's find_by_name_fast / type_text / SendInput then calls GetForegroundWindow(), which may still return the previous HWND if the OS has not yet processed the foreground change. This is the root mechanism for keystrokes landing in the wrong window between tool calls. There is no SetForegroundWindow + AttachThreadInput pattern, no SwitchToThisWindow fallback, no sleep/yield, and no post-call verification of GetForegroundWindow() == target_hwnd.

weaknesses.issue: type_text / SendInput is pure focus-dependent - no UIA ValuePattern.SetValue bypass
weaknesses.why: All text input goes through SendInput with KEYEVENTF_UNICODE, which delivers characters to whatever window has focus at the moment each SendInput call executes. There is no UIA ValuePattern.SetValue or Invoke path that would bypass focus. type_by_description does click_at then immediately calls type_text with no focus-settle wait between them. In an agent loop where the model calls ghost_focus_window then ghost_type as separate tool calls, the OS foreground change may not have propagated by the time type_text runs.

weaknesses.issue: Live binary is v0.3.0 source; v0.5.0 (OCR, NVIDIA vision, event_bus) is uncommitted working tree never built into release
weaknesses.why: target/release/ghost-mcp.exe timestamp is 2026-05-10 16:21, matching commit 801be67 (the last commit). vision.rs, ocr.rs, and event_bus.rs are listed as untracked (??) in git status - they were never committed and thus never built into the release binary. The MCP registration points directly to this stale binary. The initialize handshake returns version 0.2.0 (hardcoded in main.rs line 135). The test asserting 47 tools only runs against the current source; the live binary serves the tool list that was compiled at commit time, which is missing ghost_find_text_local, ghost_click_text_local, ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description, and the 5 v0.4 perf tools.

weaknesses.issue: Vision tools hard-fail when called via the direct 'ghost' MCP server - NVIDIA_API_KEY absent from env block
weaknesses.why: The ~/.claude.json MCP registration for the 'ghost' server has "env": {} - no NVIDIA_API_KEY and no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. pick_provider() in vision.rs checks std::env::var at call time. If neither key is present in the inherited process environment, it returns Err(GhostError::Config(...)) - there is no silent fallback or degraded mode. The comet-mcp wrapper injects NVIDIA_API_KEY correctly, but when the 'ghost' server is called directly (as it is from Claude Code's ghost MCP server), all three description-based tools will return a Config error to the model. The model sees a tool error string and has no way to know vision requires the comet wrapper.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_screenshot returns full-screen lossless PNG inline as base64 in the tool response body
weaknesses.why: ghost_screenshot calls capture_screen() which returns raw PNG bytes for the full primary monitor (typically 400KB-2MB for 1920x1080). These bytes are base64-encoded (+33%) and placed in the json response as png_base64. This entire string enters the model context window on every call. At 1080p PNG, that is 500KB-2.5MB of base64 per screenshot call. The ghost_screenshot_region alternative with foreground=true+max_dim=768+jpeg_quality=75 reduces this to 20-80KB, but the default ghost_screenshot tool has no such budget and agent loops commonly reach for the named screenshot tool first.

weaknesses.issue: 47 tools in a single MCP server strains agent tool selection - no grouping or recommended-first ordering
weaknesses.why: The tools/list response returns all 47 tools in registration order with no grouping (vision, input, inspection, utility). Several pairs have near-identical names: ghost_describe_screen vs ghost_describe_screen_fast vs ghost_describe_screen_delta; ghost_screenshot vs ghost_screenshot_region; ghost_click vs ghost_click_at vs ghost_click_by_description vs ghost_click_background vs ghost_click_text_local vs ghost_click_and_wait_for_text. Models often pick the wrong variant (e.g. ghost_screenshot instead of ghost_screenshot_region, ghost_describe_screen instead of fast variant). The descriptions note preferences (SLOW, Recommended default) but these are easy to miss with 47 tools in context.

weaknesses.issue: EventBus only hooks EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND - UIA structure/focus changes do not bump the bus
weaknesses.why: pump_thread registers SetWinEventHook for EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND only. Within-app navigation (a dialog opening, a new tab loading, a text field becoming enabled) does not fire EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND and therefore does not advance the bus seq. The find() polling loop uses bus.wait_for_change() as its backoff signal, so it will sleep for the full backoff period (25-150ms) on every retry when the target element appears within the same window. The CHANGELOG explicitly defers IUIAutomation structure-changed event handlers as too complex. This means intra-app waits regress to timed backoff rather than event-driven.

weaknesses.issue: ghost_type does UIA find then type_text with no element.click() to ensure focus before typing
weaknesses.why: The ghost_type handler calls session.find(by).await then el.type_text(text). type_text in element.rs uses keyboard::type_text which is pure SendInput. But finding an element by UIA name does not bring it into keyboard focus - only clicking it or using SetFocus does. The ghost_fill_form handler does el.click() before type_text, but the standalone ghost_type does not. If the agent calls ghost_type on a text field that is not already focused, the keystrokes go to whichever element currently has keyboard focus.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add verify-foreground retry loop in focus_window: after SetForegroundWindow, poll GetForegroundWindow() == target_hwnd up to 10 times with 10ms sleep, using AttachThreadInput if needed. Return an error if foreground was not achieved.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates the primary source of keystrokes landing in the wrong window. All type_text / SendInput calls that follow focus_window become reliable.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add el.set_focus() via IUIAutomationElement::SetFocus() before SendInput in ghost_type (and optionally in type_text). Fall back gracefully if SetFocus returns E_INVALIDARG.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Makes ghost_type focus-safe without requiring the caller to explicitly click first, eliminating a class of agent mistakes.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Commit and rebuild: run cargo build --release, replace target/release/ghost-mcp.exe, then commit vision.rs + ocr.rs + event_bus.rs + modified files as v0.5.0. Update serverInfo version to 0.5.0.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Brings the live binary in sync with source. Unlocks OCR tools, NVIDIA vision, event bus, and the 5 v0.4 perf tools (fast describe, screenshot_region, event_seq, etc.) that agents cannot currently call.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Inject NVIDIA_API_KEY (or at minimum ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) into the 'ghost' server env block in ~/.claude.json, identical to the comet server's env block.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Unblocks ghost_locate_by_description, ghost_click_by_description, ghost_type_by_description on the direct ghost server. Vision fallback becomes operational.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Replace ghost_screenshot with a budget-capped default: force foreground=true, max_dim=768, jpeg_quality=75 in the handler when no rect/max_dim/jpeg_quality is provided. Or deprecate ghost_screenshot and make ghost_screenshot_region the only capture tool.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Eliminates 500KB-2.5MB base64 blobs from model context per screenshot call. Reduces token cost by 10-50x per vision loop.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Split the 47-tool flat list into themed sub-servers or tool groups with a tool-routing meta-tool: a ghost_inspect group (find, describe_fast, get_text, list_windows) and a ghost_act group (click, type, press, hotkey, scroll). Or prefix descriptions with [INSPECT], [INPUT], [VISION], [FLOW] tags to guide model selection.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Reduces tool-selection errors in agent loops. Models consistently pick ghost_describe_screen_fast over the slow variant, ghost_screenshot_region over ghost_screenshot.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Subscribe to EVENT_OBJECT_FOCUS and EVENT_OBJECT_STATECHANGE in the event bus pump thread alongside EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND, so within-app UI changes bump the bus seq.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Converts all find() retry loops from timed backoff to event-driven wakeup for intra-app navigation, cutting per-retry latency from 25-150ms to <5ms.

improvement_opportunities.idea: Add a ghost_type_safe compound tool that does: find element, SetFocus via UIA, click_at element center, 50ms settle wait, then SendInput. Remove the manual ghost_focus_window -> ghost_type two-step from the recommended workflow.
improvement_opportunities.impact: Single tool call replaces a focus-race-prone two-call sequence. Reduces agent mistakes and makes the common case atomic across the MCP boundary.
